subject: English
sourceNotes: Primary source: local NC summary files in knowledge/english-curriculum/01_national_curriculum (eyfs.md, ks1.md, ks2.md, ks3.md, ks4.md, ks5.md). Existing apps read from /tmp/subject_apps.json (English array, 20 apps). No web or model knowledge needed beyond these.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (EYFS framework: Communication and Language + Literacy ELGs)

strands.strand: Communication and language (listening, attention, speaking)
strands.gapNote: No app develops back-and-forth talk, vocabulary, or oral sentence-building; both EYFS apps are reading/phonological-awareness only.

suggestedApps.title: Talk Partner Story Prompts
suggestedApps.concept: Project a rich scene; tap hotspots to reveal sentence-stem prompts (I can see..., I wonder why...) that model full-sentence, past/present/future talk for paired discussion.

suggestedApps.title: New Word of the Day
suggestedApps.concept: Spin a wheel of Tier-2 vocabulary with picture, sound and a use-it-in-a-sentence game children act out on screen.

strands.strand: Phonological awareness, rhyme and alliteration (Phase 1)
Comprehension: retelling and sequencing stories
Word reading: letter sounds, digraphs and sound-blending (Reception)
strands.gapNote: Phonics blending is pitched at KS1; no EYFS-level grapheme-to-sound or first-blend tool with single sounds and 10+ digraphs.

suggestedApps.title: Sound Button Blender
suggestedApps.concept: Press a sound button under each grapheme to hear it, then drag them together so the class blends the word aloud, starting with VC/CVC and the core digraphs.

strands.strand: Early writing: letter formation and writing simple phrases
strands.gapNote: No app demonstrates correct letter formation, segmenting to spell, or writing recognisable simple sentences at EYFS level.

suggestedApps.title: Magic Letter Tracer
suggestedApps.concept: Animated start-dot and directional arrows; trace lower-case letters on the board and the app replays the stroke so the class sees correct formation families.

suggestedApps.title: Hear It Spell It
suggestedApps.concept: Say a CVC word, drag sound-letter tiles into boxes (segmenting), then read the written word back to model writing simple phrases.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (NC English Years 1-2)

strands.strand: Word reading: systematic synthetic phonics and decoding
Common exception words and contractions
strands.gapNote: No app teaches reading/spelling of tricky words or apostrophe-for-omission in contractions.

suggestedApps.title: Tricky Word Detective
suggestedApps.concept: Project tricky words with the regular bits highlighted green and the irregular grapheme flagged red, so the class spots the unusual spelling part.

suggestedApps.title: Squash the Words
suggestedApps.concept: Drag two words together (do + not) and watch letters vanish into an apostrophe to build I'm, we'll, don't, with the missing letters shown.

strands.strand: Reading comprehension: inference, prediction and discussion
strands.gapNote: No app supports inference, prediction or discussing significance of title/events at KS1.

suggestedApps.title: What Happens Next?
suggestedApps.concept: Reveal a story a panel at a time; class votes on predictions, then the next panel is unmasked to test the evidence behind their guesses.

suggestedApps.title: Clue Hunter
suggestedApps.concept: Tap words/pictures in an illustrated passage that prove how a character feels, building the idea of inference from evidence.

strands.strand: Writing composition: building and sequencing sentences into narratives
Spelling and transcription: suffixes, plurals and handwriting
strands.gapNote: No app covers -s/-es plurals, un-/-ing/-ed suffixes, or letter joining/handwriting families at KS1.

suggestedApps.title: Suffix Spinner
suggestedApps.concept: Drag -s, -es, -ing, -ed, -er onto root words and watch a worked rule (double the consonant, drop the e) animate so spelling changes are visible.

strands.strand: Grammar and punctuation: capitals, full stops, question/exclamation marks, conjunctions
strands.gapNote: Sentence Builder includes capitals and full stops but not ?/!, coordination/subordination (and/but/because) or expanded noun phrases.

suggestedApps.title: Punctuation Power-Up
suggestedApps.concept: Read a sentence aloud with different end marks; the class drags the correct . ? ! and the sentence changes meaning/intonation to show statement vs question vs command.

suggestedApps.title: Join the Clauses
suggestedApps.concept: Snap two short clauses together with and/but/because/when cards and see how the joined sentence reads, building coordination and subordination.

strands.strand: Spoken language: discussion, role play and Standard English
strands.gapNote: No app supports structured talk, role play or articulating/justifying opinions at KS1.

suggestedApps.title: Show and Tell Frames
suggestedApps.concept: On-screen talk scaffolds (First..., Because..., My favourite part was...) with a turn-taking timer to structure class discussion and presentations.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (NC English Years 3-6, plus Appendix 1 spelling and Appendix 2 grammar)

strands.strand: Word reading: morphology, prefixes, suffixes and word origins
strands.gapNote: Etymology Explorer touches roots/origins but is pitched at KS3 and not focused on KS2 decoding of new words via prefixes/suffixes.

suggestedApps.title: Prefix and Suffix Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Drag super-, anti-, auto-, -ate, -ify onto root words; the app shows how meaning and pronunciation shift so pupils decode unfamiliar words.

strands.strand: Reading comprehension: inference, summarising, themes and author's language
strands.gapNote: No app teaches inference with evidence, summarising across paragraphs, identifying themes, or fact vs opinion at KS2.

suggestedApps.title: Evidence Highlighter
suggestedApps.concept: Project a passage; pupils drag inference cards (he is nervous) onto the exact words that justify them, then rate the strength of the evidence.

suggestedApps.title: Fact or Opinion Sorter
suggestedApps.concept: Statements from a non-fiction text fly in; class sorts into fact/opinion lanes and the app reveals the language signals that gave it away.

strands.strand: Writing composition: planning, paragraphing, narrative settings/characters and cohesion
Vocabulary, grammar and word classes
Sentence grammar: clauses, fronted adverbials, modal verbs, relative clauses and direct speech
strands.gapNote: No app covers fronted adverbials, relative clauses, modal verbs or inverted commas for direct speech (KS2 Appendix 2).

suggestedApps.title: Adverbial Mover
suggestedApps.concept: Drag a fronted adverbial to the start of a sentence and watch the comma appear; toggle position to feel how emphasis and flow change.

suggestedApps.title: Speech Punctuation Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Type what a character says and the app inserts inverted commas, the comma and new line, modelling correct direct-speech layout live.

strands.strand: Spelling and punctuation: homophones, apostrophes, commas, colons/semicolons and dashes
strands.gapNote: Punctuation for ambiguity is handled at KS4 (Ambiguity Explorer); no KS2-level tool for homophones, possessive apostrophes, or colon/semicolon/bullet punctuation.

suggestedApps.title: Homophone Showdown
suggestedApps.concept: A sentence with a missing word; class chooses there/their/they're and the picture/meaning updates instantly to confirm or correct.

suggestedApps.title: Punctuation Toolkit
suggestedApps.concept: Drag colon, semicolon, dash, bullet into a passage and see how each reorganises lists and clause boundaries, with a worked rule popping up.

strands.strand: Spoken language: presentations, debate and reasoned justification
strands.gapNote: No app supports formal presentation, debate or providing reasoned justifications at KS2.

suggestedApps.title: Debate Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Pick a motion; drag claim, evidence and rebuttal cards into a structured argument map the class can deliver and challenge courteously.

strands.strand: Handwriting and transcription: joins, fluency and legibility

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (NC English Years 7-9)

strands.strand: Reading whole texts: fiction, non-fiction, poetry and drama across genres and periods
strands.gapNote: No app supports reading whole books, genre/period breadth or seminal world literature; existing KS3 apps focus on isolated techniques.

suggestedApps.title: Genre and Period Map
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive timeline/genre grid where pupils place extracts and spot conventions of each form (sonnet, gothic, travel writing) by manipulating features.

strands.strand: Shakespeare and the literary heritage (two plays, pre-1914 texts)
strands.gapNote: Iambic Pentameter Scanner (KS4) supports Shakespearean verse rhythm but there is no KS3 tool for Shakespeare's language, staging or alternative interpretations.

suggestedApps.title: Stage It Yourself
suggestedApps.concept: Drag actors, lighting and blocking around a stage for a Shakespeare scene and watch how alternative staging changes the audience's interpretation.

suggestedApps.title: Shakespeare Translator
suggestedApps.concept: Slide between original Early Modern lines and modern paraphrase, with archaic words tappable for meaning, to demystify pre-1914 language.

strands.strand: Critical reading: inference, analysing writers' language and structure
Narrative and structural craft (perspective, tension, voice)
Writing for purpose and audience: stories, scripts, arguments, letters and the writing process
strands.gapNote: No app supports planning/drafting/editing across forms (argument, formal letter, script) or adapting register at KS3.

suggestedApps.title: Register Dial
suggestedApps.concept: Write one message, then turn a formality dial; the app rewrites vocabulary and tone from text-speak to formal letter so pupils see register shift.

suggestedApps.title: Draft Doctor
suggestedApps.concept: Project a weak paragraph; class drags in edits (vary sentence openings, upgrade verbs, add cohesion) and a quality meter responds live.

strands.strand: Grammar, vocabulary and word origins (etymology, morphology, register)
Spoken language: formal presentation, discussion and debate
strands.gapNote: No app supports formal presentations, debate or evaluating viewpoints at KS3.

suggestedApps.title: Rhetoric Rehearsal
suggestedApps.concept: Build a persuasive speech from claim/ethos/pathos/logos blocks, then play it back with a checklist for delivery and audience awareness.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (GCSE English Language and English Literature; English Language statutory)

strands.strand: Language Paper 1: analysing fiction language and structure (AO2)
Creative and descriptive writing (AO5/AO6)
strands.gapNote: Sentence Surgeon and Ambiguity Explorer build sentence craft but no app scaffolds a full narrative/descriptive piece with structure, tone and openings/endings.

suggestedApps.title: Descriptive Zoom
suggestedApps.concept: Pan and zoom into a picture stimulus; each zoom level prompts a sensory sentence, building a structured descriptive piece from wide shot to close detail.

strands.strand: Non-fiction reading and comparing viewpoints across time (Paper 2, AO1/AO3)
strands.gapNote: No app supports synthesising or comparing perspectives across 19th- and 20th/21st-century non-fiction texts.

suggestedApps.title: Viewpoint Comparator
suggestedApps.concept: Two non-fiction texts side by side; drag matching ideas together and the app builds a comparison grid showing how each writer's perspective and method differ.

strands.strand: Transactional/persuasive non-fiction writing (articles, speeches, letters)
Literature: Shakespeare and the 19th-century novel (close analysis and context, AO1-AO3)
strands.gapNote: Iambic Pentameter and Unreliable Narrator support method analysis but no app embeds set-text close reading with AO3 context (Macbeth, A Christmas Carol etc).

suggestedApps.title: Context Layers
suggestedApps.concept: Toggle context overlays (Jacobean kingship, Victorian poverty) onto a key extract and see how each layer changes a plausible AO3-informed reading.

suggestedApps.title: Quotation Explode
suggestedApps.concept: Tap a key quotation to fan out method, effect and context branches, modelling how to build a full analytical paragraph.

strands.strand: Literature: poetry analysis and comparison (anthology and unseen)
strands.gapNote: Iambic Pentameter Scanner covers metre only; no app supports comparing anthology poems or analysing unseen poetry (Power and Conflict, Love and Relationships).

suggestedApps.title: Poem Pair Comparer
suggestedApps.concept: Choose two anthology poems; drag shared themes/methods into a Venn-style comparison and the app suggests comparative sentence stems.

suggestedApps.title: Unseen Poem Decoder
suggestedApps.concept: Reveal an unseen poem line by line; annotate sound, imagery and structure and the app tracks how meaning accumulates toward an interpretation.

strands.strand: Spoken Language Endorsement (prepared formal presentation)
strands.gapNote: No app supports planning or rehearsing the GCSE prepared presentation in Standard English.

suggestedApps.title: Presentation Planner
suggestedApps.concept: Storyboard a 7-10 minute talk with hook, structure and Q&A prep; a Standard English checker flags informalisms before rehearsal.

strands.strand: Literature: modern prose and drama set texts (close analysis and context)

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (A-level English Literature, Language, and Language and Literature)

strands.strand: Critical interpretations and literary theory (AO5)
Comparative and synoptic essay writing across texts and time (AO4)
Close analysis of writers' craft: form, structure and language (AO2)
strands.gapNote: KS4 tools (Sentence Surgeon, Iambic Pentameter) transfer somewhat but no KS5-level tool for sustained close reading at A-level depth across genres.

suggestedApps.title: Close Reading Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Layer annotations on a dense passage (lexis, syntax, form) and the app maps how micro-choices build a sustained AO2 argument.

strands.strand: Contexts of production and reception (AO3)
strands.gapNote: Literary Theory Lens touches interpretive frameworks but no app distinguishes contexts of production vs reception or evaluates contextual claims at A-level.

suggestedApps.title: Production vs Reception
suggestedApps.concept: Split-screen a text's moment of creation against later readings; drag context evidence to each side to show how meaning is re-made over time.

strands.strand: English Language: language frameworks, variation and change (Language A-level)
strands.gapNote: No app addresses phonology/lexis/syntax/pragmatics analysis, language change over time, or social/regional variation.

suggestedApps.title: Framework Annotator
suggestedApps.concept: Feed in spoken or written data and tag features by framework (lexis, grammar, pragmatics, discourse) with effect notes, modelling systematic analysis.

suggestedApps.title: Language Change Timeline
suggestedApps.concept: Scrub a slider from Early Modern to digital English and watch sample texts morph, with class explaining drivers of variation and change.

strands.strand: English Language: child language acquisition (Language A-level)
strands.gapNote: No app covers spoken/written acquisition 0-11 or the theories (behaviourist, nativist, social-interactionist).

suggestedApps.title: Acquisition Stages Explorer
suggestedApps.concept: Drag child utterances onto a development timeline and the app matches them to stages and competing theories, revealing the evidence each predicts.

strands.strand: Non-examined assessment: independent investigation and original writing with commentary
strands.gapNote: No app scaffolds the NEA: forming a comparative title or research question, methodology/data ethics, or original writing plus commentary.

suggestedApps.title: NEA Question Forge
suggestedApps.concept: Build and stress-test a comparative-essay title or investigation hypothesis; the app flags scope, comparability and feasibility before approval.

suggestedApps.title: Data and Ethics Planner
suggestedApps.concept: Walk a language-investigation dataset through sampling, consent and anonymisation checks, modelling sound research methodology.

summary.headline: Reading-technique and analysis strands at KS3-KS5 are well covered, but spoken language/oracy across every phase, EYFS/KS1/KS2 comprehension and transcription, and the entire A-level English Language strand (frameworks, variation, child language, NEA) are the biggest gaps.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (EYFS framework: Number and Numerical Patterns ELGs)

strands.strand: Counting and cardinality
Subitising and composition of numbers to 10 (number bonds)
strands.gapNote: Number bonds via ten-frame covers composition; subitising is touched but could be deepened.

suggestedApps.title: Flash Dots
suggestedApps.concept: Dot arrangements flash for one second; class calls the total, then it reveals how the dots split into smaller groups to build conceptual subitising.

strands.strand: Comparison of quantities (more, fewer, equal)
Numerical patterns (repeating patterns, odds/evens, doubles, equal distribution)
strands.gapNote: Repeating patterns covered well; structural patterns (odds/evens, doubles, sharing equally) less explicit.

suggestedApps.title: Odd, Even and Double Machine
suggestedApps.concept: Drop a number of counters into a machine that pairs them up; if every counter has a partner it lights green for even, an odd one out lights amber, and a doubles button mirrors the set.

strands.strand: Shape, space and measure (2D/3D shapes, position, size comparison)
strands.gapNote: Shape sorting covered; positional language (under, behind, in front of) not addressed.

suggestedApps.title: Where Is Teddy?
suggestedApps.concept: Drag Teddy around a room scene; the app reads aloud the positional sentence (under the table, behind the chair) and challenges the class to place him to match a spoken instruction.

strands.strand: Early problem solving and reasoning

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (National Curriculum 2014, Years 1-2)

strands.strand: Number and place value (counting, tens and ones, comparing/ordering)
Addition and subtraction (number bonds to 20, mental and written methods, inverse)
strands.gapNote: No dedicated app builds/practises addition and subtraction fluency or number bonds within 20/100 at KS1; only word problems touch it.

suggestedApps.title: Bonds Within 20 Workshop
suggestedApps.concept: Drag counters across a part-whole model and double number line; the app shows the matching fact family and the inverse, so adding and subtracting facts appear together.

suggestedApps.title: Bridging Through Ten
suggestedApps.concept: Animated ten-frames fill and overflow to show how 8 + 5 partitions to make 10 first, with pupils dragging the split themselves.

strands.strand: Multiplication and division (2, 5, 10 tables, arrays, equal groups)
strands.gapNote: No KS1 app addresses arrays, repeated addition, or the 2/5/10 times tables.

suggestedApps.title: Array Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils drag to build rows and columns of objects; the app shows the matching multiplication and division facts and demonstrates commutativity by rotating the array.

suggestedApps.title: Skip-Count Track
suggestedApps.concept: Hop along a number track in 2s, 5s and 10s, lighting each landing number and revealing the times-table pattern.

strands.strand: Fractions (halves, quarters, thirds, equivalence of fractions of shapes/quantities)
Measurement (length, mass, capacity, temperature, money, time)
Geometry: properties of shapes and position/direction (turns)
strands.gapNote: EYFS shape sorter is pitched lower; no KS1 app for shape properties (sides, vertices, faces, symmetry) or turns/right angles.

suggestedApps.title: Shape Detective
suggestedApps.concept: Tap a 2D or 3D shape to count and highlight its sides, vertices, edges and faces, sort by property, and spot 2D faces on 3D solids.

suggestedApps.title: Turn the Robot
suggestedApps.concept: Programme a robot with quarter, half and three-quarter turns (clockwise/anti-clockwise) and watch it rotate to match a target heading.

strands.strand: Statistics (pictograms, tally charts, block diagrams)
strands.gapNote: Only a KS2 graph builder exists; nothing pitched at simple KS1 pictograms/tally/block diagrams with sorting.

suggestedApps.title: Sort and Count Pictogram
suggestedApps.concept: Drag objects into category columns to grow a live pictogram and tally chart, then answer totalling and comparing questions the app generates.

strands.strand: Reasoning and problem solving

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (National Curriculum 2014, Years 3-6)

strands.strand: Number and place value (to 10,000,000, negative numbers, rounding, Roman numerals)
strands.gapNote: No KS2 app for place value of large numbers, negative numbers across zero, rounding or Roman numerals.

suggestedApps.title: Place Value Slider
suggestedApps.concept: A draggable place-value chart to millions where pupils build numbers, see x10/divide-by-10 digit shifts, and round to any place with a snapping number line.

suggestedApps.title: Across Zero
suggestedApps.concept: A vertical number line through zero where pupils count intervals between positive and negative temperatures or floors in a lift.

strands.strand: Four operations: written methods and properties (column, long mult/division, order of operations, factors/multiples/primes)
strands.gapNote: No app demonstrates formal columnar addition/subtraction, long multiplication/division, BIDMAS or factors/multiples/primes.

suggestedApps.title: Column Method Animator
suggestedApps.concept: Step through column addition, subtraction, long multiplication and short/long division with carrying, borrowing and exchange shown digit by digit on demand.

suggestedApps.title: Factor Forest
suggestedApps.concept: Break a number into a factor tree to find primes, then highlight common factors and multiples of two numbers to reveal HCF and LCM visually.

strands.strand: Fractions, decimals and percentages
strands.gapNote: Conversion well covered; adding/subtracting fractions with different denominators and multiplying fractions could be extended.

suggestedApps.title: Fraction Calculator Bars
suggestedApps.concept: Stack and split fraction bars to add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators, with the app showing the common denominator forming live.

strands.strand: Ratio and proportion, and early algebra (formulae, linear sequences, unknowns)
strands.gapNote: KS3 sequences app partly serves linear sequences; nothing for KS2 ratio/proportion, scaling or simple formulae and missing-number algebra.

suggestedApps.title: Recipe Scaler
suggestedApps.concept: Drag a serving-size dial and watch ingredient quantities scale by ratio, with a double number line showing the multiplicative relationship.

suggestedApps.title: Mystery Number Machine
suggestedApps.concept: A function machine where pupils find the rule, express it as a simple formula, and solve missing-number problems algebraically.

strands.strand: Geometry: properties of shapes and angles
strands.gapNote: Angles and area/perimeter covered; classifying shapes and parts of a circle (radius/diameter) less explicit.

suggestedApps.title: Circle Parts Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Drag a radius around a circle to see diameter and circumference appear, naming each part and showing diameter = 2 x radius.

strands.strand: Position and direction (coordinates, translation, reflection)
Statistics (bar charts, pictograms, line graphs, pie charts, mean)
Reasoning and problem solving

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (National Curriculum 2013, Years 7-9)

strands.strand: Number (rational numbers, standard form, indices, roots, fractions/decimals/percentages multiplicatively)
strands.gapNote: No KS3 app for standard form, indices/roots, prime factorisation, or multiplicative percentage change.

suggestedApps.title: Standard Form Zoom
suggestedApps.concept: A logarithmic zoom slider from atoms to galaxies where each scale shows the number in ordinary and standard form, with operations on powers of ten.

suggestedApps.title: Percentage Multiplier Machine
suggestedApps.concept: Apply increase/decrease multipliers to a bar and chain them to model compound change, reverse percentages and simple interest.

strands.strand: Algebra (notation, manipulation, expanding/factorising, equations and inequalities)
strands.gapNote: Simultaneous equations are visualised, but expanding/factorising, collecting like terms, and solving linear equations/inequalities are not covered.

suggestedApps.title: Algebra Tiles Workbench
suggestedApps.concept: Drag algebra tiles to collect like terms, expand single brackets and factorise, with the symbolic expression updating as tiles move.

suggestedApps.title: Balance Equation Solver
suggestedApps.concept: A two-pan balance where pupils perform the same operation on both sides to isolate x, visualising why the balance method works.

strands.strand: Algebra: sequences and graphs (nth term, linear/quadratic graphs)
strands.gapNote: Sequences and nth term covered; plotting/interpreting linear graphs (gradient/intercept) less explicit.

suggestedApps.title: Line Equation Explorer
suggestedApps.concept: Drag a line on a grid to change gradient and intercept, watching y = mx + c update live and reading gradient as a rate of change.

strands.strand: Ratio, proportion and rates of change
strands.gapNote: No KS3 app for ratio sharing, direct/inverse proportion, or interpreting gradient as a rate of change.

suggestedApps.title: Ratio Table Tool
suggestedApps.concept: A scaling ratio table and double number line that pupils stretch to solve sharing, recipe and best-buy problems multiplicatively.

suggestedApps.title: Direct vs Inverse Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Compare two live graphs (y proportional to x and y proportional to 1/x) by dragging one variable and watching the other respond.

strands.strand: Geometry and measures (area/volume, angles, Pythagoras, congruence/similarity, constructions)
strands.gapNote: Transformations covered; area/volume of prisms, angle reasoning, Pythagoras and ruler-and-compass constructions need KS3-pitched apps.

suggestedApps.title: Pythagoras Proof Squares
suggestedApps.concept: Drag the squares on the sides of a right triangle and rearrange the area pieces to show a-squared + b-squared = c-squared dynamically.

suggestedApps.title: Angle Reasoning Detective
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils apply angle facts (on a line, around a point, in parallel lines) to find missing angles, with each step justified by a named rule.

strands.strand: Probability
strands.gapNote: Experiments covered; sample space/tree/Venn diagrams for combined events could be added.

suggestedApps.title: Sample Space Grid
suggestedApps.concept: Build a two-dice or two-event grid and tree diagram where outcomes auto-populate and probabilities of events compute as the class selects regions.

strands.strand: Statistics (averages, spread, charts, the statistical cycle)
strands.gapNote: KS2 graph builder includes mean/median/mode/range; pitched slightly low for KS3 grouped data and comparison of distributions.

suggestedApps.title: Compare Distributions
suggestedApps.concept: Two live data sets shown as box plots and bar charts where pupils drag values and watch averages, range and shape change to compare them.

strands.strand: Reasoning and problem solving

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (GCSE 9-1, statutory NC programme of study)

strands.strand: Number (surds, indices, standard form, bounds and accuracy)
strands.gapNote: No GCSE app for surds, laws of indices, standard form operations, or error intervals/bounds.

suggestedApps.title: Surds Simplifier
suggestedApps.concept: Drag a square root onto a factor grid to pull out perfect squares and rationalise denominators, showing each manipulation step visually.

suggestedApps.title: Bounds and Error Bars
suggestedApps.concept: Set a measurement and rounding degree, then see the upper and lower bound interval and how bounds combine in calculations.

strands.strand: Algebra: quadratics, functions and graphs
strands.gapNote: Quadratic graphs covered well; completing the square, cubic/reciprocal/exponential graphs less explicit.

suggestedApps.title: Graph Family Gallery
suggestedApps.concept: Toggle and morph between linear, quadratic, cubic, reciprocal and exponential graphs with sliders, matching equations to shapes.

strands.strand: Algebra: equations, inequalities, simultaneous equations, rearranging
strands.gapNote: Simultaneous (linear) covered; quadratic equations (factorising, formula, completing square), inequalities on graphs and rearranging formulae not covered.

suggestedApps.title: Quadratic Solver Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Enter a quadratic and watch factorising, completing the square and the formula solve it side by side, linked to where the curve crosses the x-axis.

suggestedApps.title: Change the Subject
suggestedApps.concept: Animated rearranging where each inverse operation peels away from both sides to make a chosen variable the subject of a formula.

strands.strand: Ratio, proportion and rates of change (percentages, growth/decay, compound units)
strands.gapNote: No GCSE app for compound growth/decay, compound measures (speed/density/pressure) or proportion problems.

suggestedApps.title: Compound Growth Plotter
suggestedApps.concept: Slide an interest/decay rate and see a balance grow or shrink as a multiplier chain and an exponential curve, contrasting simple and compound.

suggestedApps.title: Compound Units Converter
suggestedApps.concept: Drag sliders for distance/time, mass/volume and force/area to compute speed, density and pressure with live unit tracking.

strands.strand: Geometry and measures: mensuration, trigonometry, Pythagoras, vectors
strands.gapNote: Trigonometry, vectors and transformations covered; mensuration of cones/spheres/pyramids and circle theorems not addressed.

suggestedApps.title: Circle Theorems Playground
suggestedApps.concept: Drag points around a circle to discover and verify circle theorems live (angle at centre, angles in same segment, cyclic quadrilaterals).

strands.strand: Probability (combined events, tree/Venn diagrams, conditional)
strands.gapNote: Simulator covers experiments; tree diagrams, Venn diagrams and conditional probability at GCSE not explicitly built.

suggestedApps.title: Tree Diagram Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Construct branching tree diagrams where probabilities auto-multiply along branches and add across, including with/without replacement.

suggestedApps.title: Conditional Venn Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Drag set sizes in a two- or three-set Venn diagram and compute P(A given B) live from the regions.

strands.strand: Statistics (sampling, charts, averages, spread, histograms, cumulative frequency)
strands.gapNote: Only a primary-pitched graph builder exists; GCSE histograms with unequal intervals, cumulative frequency and box plots are not covered.

suggestedApps.title: Histogram and Frequency Density
suggestedApps.concept: Drag class-interval widths and frequencies to see bars rescale by frequency density, contrasting with a naive bar chart.

suggestedApps.title: Cumulative Frequency and Box Plots
suggestedApps.concept: Build a cumulative frequency curve from a table and read off median and quartiles to generate a linked box plot.

strands.strand: Problem solving and mathematical reasoning (AO2/AO3)

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (A-level Maths/Further Maths/Core Maths, DfE subject content)

strands.strand: Pure: algebra, functions, proof, sequences and series
strands.gapNote: Sequences/series touched (pitched lower); A-level functions (domain/range/composite/inverse, transformations, partial fractions, binomial expansion) and proof not covered.

suggestedApps.title: Function Transformer
suggestedApps.concept: Apply translations, stretches and reflections to f(x) with sliders, showing the algebraic change and domain/range and composite/inverse mapping diagrams.

suggestedApps.title: Proof Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Construct proof by deduction, exhaustion and counterexample by ordering justified statements, with the app checking logical validity.

strands.strand: Pure: coordinate geometry and trigonometry (radians, identities)
strands.gapNote: Unit-circle trig covered; radian measure, trig identities/equations, compound angles and parametric/circle coordinate geometry less explicit.

suggestedApps.title: Radians and Identities Lab
suggestedApps.concept: A unit circle marked in radians where pupils sweep an angle to verify identities and solve trig equations across a chosen interval.

strands.strand: Pure: differentiation and integration (calculus)
Pure: numerical methods and vectors
strands.gapNote: 2D vectors covered (GCSE-level); numerical methods (change of sign, Newton-Raphson, trapezium rule) and A-level vector geometry not covered.

suggestedApps.title: Newton-Raphson Visualiser
suggestedApps.concept: Tap a starting point and watch tangents iterate towards a root, with change-of-sign intervals and the trapezium rule shown as an alternative.

strands.strand: Statistics: sampling, data presentation, correlation and regression
strands.gapNote: No A-level-pitched app for sampling methods, standard deviation, scatter/PMCC or regression lines; only primary graph builder exists.

suggestedApps.title: Correlation and Regression Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Drag points on a scatter plot to watch PMCC and the least-squares regression line update, with residuals and the large data set in view.

strands.strand: Statistics: probability distributions and hypothesis testing
Mechanics: kinematics, forces and Newton's laws, moments
strands.gapNote: Kinematics (SUVAT) covered; forces and Newton's laws, connected particles/friction/inclined planes and moments not covered.

suggestedApps.title: Force Diagram Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Drag forces onto a body on a slope or pulley system, resolve them into components and apply F=ma to find acceleration and tension live.

suggestedApps.title: Moments and Balance
suggestedApps.concept: Hang masses on a pivoted beam and adjust distances to reach equilibrium, computing moments and centre of mass interactively.

strands.strand: Core Maths: financial maths, estimation, critical analysis of data
strands.gapNote: No app supports the Core Maths pathway (personal finance/APR, Fermi estimation, evaluating statistical claims in the media).

suggestedApps.title: Personal Finance Modeller
suggestedApps.concept: Adjust salary, tax, loan APR and compound savings to see take-home pay and debt/savings projections, exposing how interest and inflation bite.

suggestedApps.title: Stats in the News
suggestedApps.concept: Present real media graphs with truncated axes or misleading scales and let the class fix them to reveal the true picture.

strands.strand: Mathematical modelling, problem solving and synthesis
Further Maths Core Pure: complex numbers, matrices and polar curves
Further Maths Decision: networks, algorithms and linear programming
Further Maths Statistics: further distributions and hypothesis tests

sourceNotes: Curriculum content extracted from local knowledge files in knowledge/maths-curriculum/01_national_curriculum (eyfs.md, ks1.md, ks2.md, ks3.md, ks4.md, ks5.md). Existing apps read from /tmp/subject_apps.json (Mathematics, 41 apps). No web or external sources used.
subject: Mathematics

summary.headline: Strong coverage of EYFS/KS1 number, calculus and A-level distributions, plus a complete reasoning/problem-solving thread at every phase, but the biggest gaps are the formal written number and algebra core of KS2-KS4 (column/long methods, factors and primes, expanding/factorising, surds, indices, standard form) and KS3-KS5 ratio/proportion, with no Core Maths or A-level mechanics-forces/statistics support.

subject: Science
sourceNotes: Primary source: local NC summary files at knowledge/science-curriculum/01_national_curriculum/ (eyfs.md, ks1.md, ks2.md, ks3.md, ks4.md, ks5.md). KS3/KS4/KS5 content sections supplemented with model knowledge of the statutory NC programmes of study (KS3) and standard GCSE/A-level subject content (Biology, Chemistry, Physics) where the local files focused on regulatory/pedagogical framing rather than listing every topic. Existing apps from /tmp/subject_apps.json (Science key, 20 apps).

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (Understanding the World - Natural World ELG; science not a discrete subject)

strands.strand: The natural world and living things (plants, animals, life processes)
strands.gapNote: Plant Growth Diary (KS1) touches plants but there is no EYFS-pitched explore-living-things app for animals/minibeasts/observation

suggestedApps.title: Minibeast Hunt
suggestedApps.concept: Tap and reveal creatures in a garden scene, drag each to 'has legs / has wings / lives underground' baskets to sort living things by features

suggestedApps.title: What Plants Need to Grow
suggestedApps.concept: Class controls water, light and warmth sliders on three pots and watches over days which seed sprouts, building the EYFS idea of growth needs

strands.strand: Seasons, weather and changes over time
Senses and exploring the world through observation
Materials and physical processes (floating, melting, movement)
strands.gapNote: No EYFS app exploring everyday materials, floating/sinking or melting through play

suggestedApps.title: Float or Sink Tank
suggestedApps.concept: Drag everyday objects into a water tank and watch them float or sink, then sort them into two hoops as a class prediction game

suggestedApps.title: Ice and Melt Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Move a sun or warm hand over frozen objects and watch ice melt in real time, introducing simple cause and effect

strands.strand: Care for and respect the natural environment
strands.gapNote: No app on caring for living things or the local environment

suggestedApps.title: Look After the Garden
suggestedApps.concept: Children tap to water, weed and feed birds in a garden scene, learning how to care for living things and the environment

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Plants (structure, growth, common plants and trees)
Animals, including humans (groups, body parts, senses, basic needs)
strands.gapNote: Senses Explorer covers the five senses but nothing covers animal groups (fish/birds/mammals), diet (herbivore/carnivore) or naming body parts at KS1

suggestedApps.title: Animal Sorting Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Drag animals into fish, amphibian, reptile, bird and mammal pens and toggle their diet to herbivore, carnivore or omnivore

suggestedApps.title: Label the Body
suggestedApps.concept: Drag-and-drop labels onto a human figure for body parts and link each to its matching sense

strands.strand: Everyday materials and their properties / uses
Living things and their habitats (Year 2: alive/dead/never-alive, microhabitats, food chains)
strands.gapNote: Food Chain Builder (KS2) is pitched higher; no KS1 app on alive/dead/never-been-alive or matching animals to habitats

suggestedApps.title: Alive, Dead or Never Alive?
suggestedApps.concept: Sort objects (a tree, a rock, a fallen leaf, a toy) into three bins using the MRS GREN life processes as prompts

suggestedApps.title: Match the Habitat
suggestedApps.concept: Drag animals to the right habitat (pond, woodland, ocean, desert) and reveal a simple who-eats-whom chain for that place

strands.strand: Seasonal changes (weather, day length across seasons)
strands.gapNote: Seasons Wheel (EYFS) covers this well and remains valid at Year 1

strands.strand: Working scientifically (observing, questioning, simple tests, sorting)
strands.gapNote: Sorting is embedded in content apps but there is no dedicated enquiry-skills app (asking questions, simple comparative tests)

suggestedApps.title: Test It! Fair Test Maker
suggestedApps.concept: Pick one thing to change and one to measure in a class-built simple test (e.g. which paper towel soaks up most), keeping everything else the same

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Plants (function of parts, water transport, reproduction)
strands.gapNote: Plant Growth Diary is KS1; Photosynthesis app is KS4. No KS2 app on parts of a flower, pollination or water transport in stems

suggestedApps.title: Inside a Flower
suggestedApps.concept: Dissect a virtual flower, label stamen/carpel/petal and run a pollination-to-seed animation

suggestedApps.title: Water Up the Stem
suggestedApps.concept: Add coloured dye to water and watch it travel up a celery stalk, with a timer to compare conditions

strands.strand: Animals including humans (nutrition, skeletons, digestion, circulation, life cycles)
strands.gapNote: No KS2 app on the human body: digestive system, skeleton/muscles, teeth, or the circulatory system

suggestedApps.title: Journey Through Digestion
suggestedApps.concept: Send food on an animated trip through mouth, stomach and intestines, tapping each organ to learn its job

suggestedApps.title: Heartbeat Circuit
suggestedApps.concept: Pump a beating heart and watch blood flow around the body, speeding up with exercise to show pulse change

strands.strand: Living things and habitats (classification, environmental change, life cycles)
strands.gapNote: Food Chain Builder covers feeding relationships well but not classification keys or life cycles (mammal/insect/amphibian/bird)

suggestedApps.title: Build a Classification Key
suggestedApps.concept: Drag yes/no questions to branch a set of organisms into a working dichotomous key the class tests live

suggestedApps.title: Life Cycle Spinner
suggestedApps.concept: Reorder cards into correct cycles for a frog, butterfly, bird and mammal, then play each as an animation

strands.strand: Rocks, soils and fossils
States of matter and the water cycle
strands.gapNote: No KS2 app on solids/liquids/gases, melting/freezing or evaporation and the water cycle (KS3 Matter app is pitched higher)

suggestedApps.title: Heat It, Cool It
suggestedApps.concept: Drag a thermometer up and down to melt, freeze, evaporate and condense a substance, watching particles spread out and pack in

suggestedApps.title: Build the Water Cycle
suggestedApps.concept: Place sun, clouds, rain and rivers on a landscape and run an animated cycle showing evaporation and condensation

strands.strand: Properties and changes of materials (solubility, separating, reversible/irreversible)
strands.gapNote: Materials Sorter (KS1) covers properties; separating mixtures (filtering/evaporating) and reversible changes are not deeply covered but the strand is broadly touched

strands.strand: Light and shadows
strands.gapNote: No KS2 app on light travelling in straight lines, reflection or shadow formation (EM Spectrum app is KS3)

suggestedApps.title: Shadow Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Move a light source around an object and watch the shadow change size and direction, with a sundial challenge

suggestedApps.title: Bounce the Light Beam
suggestedApps.concept: Aim a ray of light at mirrors to hit a target, showing reflection and straight-line travel

strands.strand: Forces and magnets (gravity, friction, air/water resistance, magnetism)
strands.gapNote: Forces & Motion Simulator is KS3 (F=ma); KS2 needs simpler exploration of gravity, friction, levers and magnetic attraction/poles

suggestedApps.title: Magnet Playground
suggestedApps.concept: Drag magnets near materials to test attraction, flip poles to feel push/pull and find which materials are magnetic

suggestedApps.title: Ramp and Friction Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Change surface roughness and ramp angle and race a toy car, measuring how far it travels

strands.strand: Electricity (simple circuits, switches, conductors)
strands.gapNote: Circuit Builder is pitched at KS3; a simpler KS2 version (bulb lights / does not light, switches, conductors vs insulators) would fit the programme better

suggestedApps.title: Light the Bulb
suggestedApps.concept: Drag cells, wires, bulbs and a switch to complete a circuit, testing everyday objects as conductors or insulators

strands.strand: Sound (vibration, pitch, volume)
strands.gapNote: No app on how sound is made by vibrations, travels, and changes pitch/volume

suggestedApps.title: See the Sound
suggestedApps.concept: Pluck strings and bang drums to watch vibrations and a live waveform, with sliders for pitch and volume

strands.strand: Earth and space (solar system, day/night, Moon phases)
strands.gapNote: No KS2 app on the heliocentric solar system, Earth's rotation causing day/night, or Moon phases

suggestedApps.title: Spin the Earth
suggestedApps.concept: Rotate the Earth and orbit it round the Sun to show day/night and the year, with a movable observer experiencing sunrise

suggestedApps.title: Moon Phase Maker
suggestedApps.concept: Drag the Moon around Earth and watch the phase change, matching each position to its night-sky view

strands.strand: Evolution and inheritance (adaptation, variation, fossils as evidence)
strands.gapNote: No KS2 app on adaptation, inherited variation or evolution evidence (Punnett app is KS4 genetics)

suggestedApps.title: Survive the Island
suggestedApps.concept: Change an environment (cold, predators) and select which beak/fur traits survive over generations to show adaptation

strands.strand: Working scientifically at KS2 (variables, fair tests, recording, conclusions)
strands.gapNote: No dedicated enquiry-skills app for planning fair tests, choosing variables, taking measurements and drawing conclusions

suggestedApps.title: Fair Test Designer
suggestedApps.concept: Set up an investigation by choosing the variable to change, what to measure and what to keep the same, then auto-plot the results

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Biology: cells, structure and function of living organisms
Biology: material cycles and energy (photosynthesis, respiration, nutrition)
strands.gapNote: Photosynthesis & Respiration app is pitched at KS4 (compensation point); a KS3-level version plus digestion/nutrition is missing

suggestedApps.title: Leaf Factory (KS3)
suggestedApps.concept: Adjust light and CO2 on a leaf and watch glucose and oxygen produced, with a simple word equation building live

strands.strand: Biology: interactions, interdependence and health (ecosystems, gas exchange, reproduction, drugs/diet)
strands.gapNote: Food chains touched only at KS2 level; no KS3 app on gas exchange, human reproduction, diet/drugs or interdependence

suggestedApps.title: Ecosystem Balancer
suggestedApps.concept: Adjust populations of producers, predators and prey and watch a pyramid of numbers stabilise or crash

suggestedApps.title: Gas Exchange Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Animate breathing through the lungs and diffusion at the alveoli, with sliders for breathing rate during exercise

strands.strand: Biology: genetics and evolution (variation, inheritance, natural selection)
strands.gapNote: Punnett Square app is KS4-level genetics; KS3 needs an accessible variation and natural-selection introduction

suggestedApps.title: Variation Survey
suggestedApps.concept: Collect class data on continuous and discontinuous traits and auto-plot histograms to show variation

strands.strand: Chemistry: particulate nature of matter and states (particle model, changes of state, diffusion)
strands.gapNote: Local KS3 file lists Matter (particle model, Brownian motion, diffusion, pressure) but no app simulates particles across states

suggestedApps.title: Particle Theatre
suggestedApps.concept: Heat and cool a substance and watch particles change arrangement and energy across solid, liquid and gas, with pressure and diffusion demos

strands.strand: Chemistry: atoms, elements, compounds, mixtures and the periodic table
strands.gapNote: Periodic Table Explorer is KS4; no KS3 app on elements vs compounds vs mixtures or separating techniques

suggestedApps.title: Element, Compound or Mixture?
suggestedApps.concept: Build substances from atom tiles and sort what you make, then run chromatography/distillation to separate mixtures

strands.strand: Chemistry: chemical reactions, energetics and the Earth/atmosphere
strands.gapNote: No KS3 app on word/symbol equations, conservation of mass, acids and bases, exothermic/endothermic, or the carbon cycle/atmosphere

suggestedApps.title: Reaction Balancer
suggestedApps.concept: Drag atoms to balance an equation and weigh products to prove conservation of mass with a live mass readout

suggestedApps.title: Acid and Alkali Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Mix acids and alkalis, watch indicator colour and pH change, and reach neutralisation on a moving pH scale

strands.strand: Physics: energy (stores, transfers, conservation, efficiency)
Physics: motion and forces (speed, F=ma, pressure, balanced/unbalanced)
Physics: waves (sound, light, the EM spectrum)
strands.gapNote: EM Spectrum Explorer covers light/waves well; sound waves specifically are lighter but the strand is broadly addressed

strands.strand: Physics: electricity and magnetism (circuits, current/voltage, magnets)
Physics: space (gravity, solar system, seasons, the universe)
strands.gapNote: Local KS3 file details Space Physics (g, weight, solar system, seasons/tilt, light year) but no app covers it (Electric Fields is KS5)

suggestedApps.title: Weight on Other Worlds
suggestedApps.concept: Drop an object on different planets, changing g, and compare weight vs mass with a live W = m g readout

suggestedApps.title: Tilt and Seasons
suggestedApps.concept: Tilt the Earth and orbit the Sun to show how axial tilt drives seasons and day length in each hemisphere

strands.strand: Working scientifically (planning, apparatus, analysis, evaluation)
strands.gapNote: No KS3 enquiry-skills app for variables, accuracy/precision, drawing/interpreting graphs and evaluating method

suggestedApps.title: Investigation Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Plan a method, choose apparatus and variables, collect simulated data with random error, then plot and evaluate reliability

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (GCSE Combined/Triple Science against DfE subject content)

strands.strand: Biology: cell biology, transport and microscopy
strands.gapNote: Cells demo is pitched at KS3; no GCSE-level treatment of mitosis, stem cells, diffusion/osmosis/active transport or magnification calculations

suggestedApps.title: Osmosis Visking Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Set solute concentrations either side of a membrane and watch water move, plotting percentage mass change against concentration

suggestedApps.title: Cell Cycle and Mitosis
suggestedApps.concept: Step through the cell cycle stages with chromosomes separating and calculate magnification from a scale bar

strands.strand: Biology: organisation, enzymes, transport systems and disease
strands.gapNote: Enzyme Kinetics covers enzymes strongly; the wider strand (digestive/circulatory systems, communicable/non-communicable disease, immunity) is not covered

suggestedApps.title: Heart and Blood Vessels
suggestedApps.concept: Trace double circulation, label vessels and adjust heart rate and stroke volume to see cardiac output change

suggestedApps.title: Pathogen Defence
suggestedApps.concept: Run a pathogen invasion and deploy the immune response, antibiotics and vaccination to compare outcomes

strands.strand: Biology: bioenergetics (photosynthesis and respiration)
Biology: homeostasis and response (nervous/hormonal control)
strands.gapNote: No app on the nervous system/reflex arc, blood glucose control, the menstrual cycle hormones or thermoregulation

suggestedApps.title: Blood Sugar Balance
suggestedApps.concept: Eat and exercise and watch insulin/glucagon return blood glucose to normal, modelling type 1 and 2 diabetes

suggestedApps.title: Reflex Arc Animator
suggestedApps.concept: Fire a stimulus and trace the impulse through receptor, sensory, relay and motor neurones to the effector

strands.strand: Biology: inheritance, variation and evolution
Biology: ecology (ecosystems, cycles, biodiversity)
strands.gapNote: Food chains only addressed at KS2; no GCSE app on quadrat/transect sampling, carbon/water cycles or human impact on biodiversity

suggestedApps.title: Quadrat Sampler
suggestedApps.concept: Throw quadrats across a virtual field, count species and calculate population estimates and distribution

suggestedApps.title: Carbon Cycle Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: Adjust deforestation and emissions and watch carbon flow between atmosphere, biosphere and oceans over time

strands.strand: Chemistry: atomic structure and the periodic table
Chemistry: bonding, structure and properties of matter
strands.gapNote: No app on ionic/covalent/metallic bonding, dot-and-cross diagrams or structure-property links

suggestedApps.title: Bonding Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Drag electrons to form ionic and covalent bonds, generate dot-and-cross diagrams and predict the resulting properties

strands.strand: Chemistry: quantitative chemistry (moles, equations, calculations)
strands.gapNote: No app on conservation of mass, the mole, reacting masses, concentration or percentage yield

suggestedApps.title: Mole Calculator Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Balance an equation then convert between mass, moles and particles with a live reacting-mass and yield calculator

strands.strand: Chemistry: chemical changes and energy changes
strands.gapNote: No app on acids/bases/neutralisation, reactivity series, electrolysis or exothermic/endothermic profiles

suggestedApps.title: Electrolysis Cell
suggestedApps.concept: Electrolyse molten and aqueous solutions, watch ions migrate and predict products at each electrode

suggestedApps.title: Energy Profile Plotter
suggestedApps.concept: Run exothermic and endothermic reactions and draw reaction profiles with activation energy and catalyst effects

strands.strand: Chemistry: rates, equilibrium and organic chemistry
strands.gapNote: Enzyme kinetics is biology; Organic Mechanisms is KS5. No GCSE app on rate of reaction (collision theory), Le Chatelier equilibrium or crude oil/fractional distillation and alkanes/alkenes

suggestedApps.title: Rate of Reaction Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Change temperature, concentration, surface area and catalyst and measure gas volume against time, linking to collision theory

suggestedApps.title: Crude Oil Fractionator
suggestedApps.concept: Heat crude oil in a fractionating column and drag fractions to their uses, building the alkane homologous series

strands.strand: Chemistry: chemical analysis, atmosphere and using resources
strands.gapNote: No app on purity/chromatography, gas/flame tests, the evolving atmosphere/climate change or sustainable resources

suggestedApps.title: Chromatography and Tests
suggestedApps.concept: Run paper chromatography, calculate Rf and perform flame and gas tests to identify unknown samples

suggestedApps.title: Changing Atmosphere
suggestedApps.concept: Wind through Earth's history adjusting volcanic and photosynthetic activity to see oxygen and CO2 levels evolve

strands.strand: Physics: energy, electricity and the particle model
strands.gapNote: Energy and circuits apps are pitched at KS3; GCSE needs power/efficiency calculations, I-V characteristics, mains electricity and specific heat/latent heat

suggestedApps.title: I-V Characteristics
suggestedApps.concept: Vary voltage across a resistor, filament lamp and diode and plot live I-V curves to compare components

suggestedApps.title: Specific Heat Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Heat different masses and materials, plot temperature against energy and calculate specific heat capacity and latent heat

strands.strand: Physics: forces and motion (Newton's laws, momentum, stopping distance)
strands.gapNote: Forces & Motion is KS3-level; GCSE needs distance-time/velocity-time graphs, momentum conservation and stopping/thinking/braking distances

suggestedApps.title: Motion Graph Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Drive a car and generate distance-time and velocity-time graphs live, reading off speed, acceleration and area under the curve

suggestedApps.title: Stopping Distance Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: Change speed, reaction time and road conditions and watch thinking, braking and total stopping distance update

strands.strand: Physics: waves and the electromagnetic spectrum
Physics: magnetism, electromagnetism and atomic/nuclear physics
strands.gapNote: Radioactive Decay covers half-life well; magnetism/electromagnetism (motor effect, induction, transformers) is not covered

suggestedApps.title: Motor Effect Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Place a current-carrying wire in a magnetic field, use Fleming's left-hand rule and watch a model motor spin

suggestedApps.title: Transformer Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Change turns on primary and secondary coils to step voltage up and down with a live ratio calculation

strands.strand: Working scientifically and required practicals
strands.gapNote: Several content apps double as practical demos but there is no app explicitly framed around the GCSE required practicals and their evaluation

suggestedApps.title: Required Practical Trainer
suggestedApps.concept: Run a virtual required practical, identify variables and errors, then answer exam-style method and analysis questions

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (A-level Biology, Chemistry, Physics against DfE 2014 subject content)

strands.strand: Biology: biological molecules, cells and exchange
strands.gapNote: Transcription and enzyme kinetics are relevant but molecules (proteins/lipids/carbohydrates), membrane transport and gas/water exchange surfaces are not covered at A-level depth

suggestedApps.title: Membrane Transport Explorer
suggestedApps.concept: Model diffusion, facilitated diffusion, osmosis and active transport across a membrane with adjustable gradients and ATP

strands.strand: Biology: genetics, evolution, biodiversity and ecosystems
strands.gapNote: Punnett app supports monohybrid genetics; A-level dihybrid crosses, chi-squared, Hardy-Weinberg, gene pools and energy/nutrient cycling are missing

suggestedApps.title: Hardy-Weinberg Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: Set allele frequencies and selection pressures and watch a population evolve, calculating p and q each generation

suggestedApps.title: Dihybrid and Chi-squared
suggestedApps.concept: Run a dihybrid cross, tally offspring ratios and compute chi-squared against expected values

strands.strand: Biology: response, homeostasis and control (nervous, hormonal, muscles)
strands.gapNote: No A-level app on action potentials, synapses, kidney/osmoregulation or muscle contraction

suggestedApps.title: Action Potential Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Trigger a neurone and watch ion channels open to generate and propagate an action potential, plotting membrane potential live

strands.strand: Chemistry: physical chemistry (atomic structure, energetics, kinetics, equilibria, thermodynamics, electrochemistry)
strands.gapNote: Local KS5 file lists this in full (Hess, Maxwell-Boltzmann, Kc/Kp, Born-Haber, electrode potentials, pH/buffers) but no app addresses it

suggestedApps.title: Maxwell-Boltzmann Explorer
suggestedApps.concept: Adjust temperature and catalyst and watch the distribution shift relative to activation energy, linking to rate

suggestedApps.title: pH and Buffer Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Titrate strong/weak acids and bases, plot the pH curve and model buffer action with live pH and pKa readouts

strands.strand: Chemistry: inorganic chemistry (periodicity, groups, transition metals)
strands.gapNote: Periodic Table Explorer (KS4) gives trends; A-level Group 2/7, Period 3 oxides and transition-metal complexes/colours are not covered

suggestedApps.title: Transition Metal Complexes
suggestedApps.concept: Swap ligands around a central ion, predict shape and coordination number and watch the solution colour change

strands.strand: Chemistry: organic chemistry and analysis (mechanisms, synthesis, spectroscopy)
strands.gapNote: Organic Reaction Mechanisms covers mechanisms strongly; instrumental analysis (NMR, IR, mass spec) and multi-step synthesis routes are not covered

suggestedApps.title: Spectra Detective
suggestedApps.concept: Match IR, mass spec and proton/carbon NMR spectra to candidate molecules to deduce an unknown structure

strands.strand: Physics: mechanics, materials and waves
strands.gapNote: Wave interference and forces (KS3) are relevant but A-level projectiles, moments, momentum, materials (stress-strain/Young modulus) and SHM are not covered

suggestedApps.title: Projectile and Moments Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Launch projectiles and balance beams, resolving vectors and calculating range, moments and centre of mass live

suggestedApps.title: Stress-Strain Tester
suggestedApps.concept: Stretch different materials to plot stress against strain and read off Young modulus, elastic limit and breaking point

strands.strand: Physics: electricity, fields (gravitational, electric, magnetic) and capacitance
strands.gapNote: Electric Fields app covers fields well; gravitational fields and capacitance are lighter but the fields strand is broadly addressed

strands.strand: Physics: nuclear, particle physics and astrophysics/options
strands.gapNote: Decay Simulator (KS4) gives half-life; A-level particle physics (standard model, quarks), nuclear energetics and the option topics (astrophysics etc.) are not covered

suggestedApps.title: Particle Zoo
suggestedApps.concept: Build hadrons from quarks, conserve charge/baryon number in interactions and read Feynman-style diagrams

strands.strand: Practical endorsement and CPAC skills (all three sciences)
strands.gapNote: No app framed around the A-level practical endorsement, CPAC criteria, uncertainties or error propagation

suggestedApps.title: Uncertainty and Error Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Take repeated virtual measurements, calculate mean, uncertainty and percentage error and propagate it through a calculation

summary.headline: Coverage is strongest at KS3 physics and KS4 named topics, but primary content (KS2 human body, light, sound, space, water cycle) and the entire chemistry-as-reactions thread plus working-scientifically/practical skills at every key stage are the biggest gaps.

subject: History
sourceNotes: Curriculum content extracted from local knowledge files at /Users/nathanaelparnell/CCP2/curriculum-expert-web/knowledge/history-curriculum/01_national_curriculum (eyfs.md, ks1.md, ks2.md, ks3.md, ks4.md, alevel.md). Existing apps read from /tmp/subject_apps.json (key: History, 20 apps). No web or external model knowledge needed; all strands traced to statutory NC History programmes of study (DfE 2013), GCSE subject content (DFE-00346-2014) and GCE A-level subject content (2014).

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (Understanding the World: Past and Present ELG; history is not a discrete subject in EYFS)

strands.strand: Past and present: similarities and differences over time
Chronology and sequencing personal/family events
Lives and roles of people around us (community, jobs, then and now)
strands.gapNote: Then-and-Now focuses on objects, not on people's roles/jobs in society which the Past and Present ELG also requires.

suggestedApps.title: Who Helps Us, Then and Now
suggestedApps.concept: Tap a community helper (nurse, postie, baker) to flip between an old photo and a modern one, with a read-aloud sentence comparing what their day looked like.

strands.strand: Understanding the past through stories and storytelling
strands.gapNote: No app turns a story/storybook setting into a past-vs-present exploration as the ELG specifies.

suggestedApps.title: Story Time Traveller
suggestedApps.concept: Project a familiar tale (e.g. a castle story); children drag characters into a 'long ago' or 'today' room and the app narrates why each clue (candles, horses, swords) shows it happened long ago.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Chronology and time vocabulary (sequencing, past/present/before/after)
strands.gapNote: Old/New Sort gives a two-way past/now split but no ordered timeline or graded time vocabulary (long ago, recently, decade).

suggestedApps.title: When Did It Happen? Class Timeline
suggestedApps.concept: Whole-class drag of photos (grandparents' school, Victorian street, today) onto a left-to-right time ribbon; the app snaps each and reads the matching time phrase aloud.

strands.strand: Changes within living memory (toys, homes, schools, transport)
Events beyond living memory significant nationally/globally (Great Fire of London, first flight, Moon landing)
strands.gapNote: No app teaches a named significant past event; this is a core KS1 statutory area.

suggestedApps.title: The Great Fire of London: 5 Days
suggestedApps.concept: Animated map of 1666 London; pupils tap to advance each day, watch the fire spread along timber houses, and reveal a Pepys diary clue explaining why it spread and what changed afterwards.

suggestedApps.title: Why We Remember
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive calendar where children tap an anniversary (Bonfire Night, Remembrance Day, first Moon landing) to unlock a short illustrated 'what happened and why it matters' card.

strands.strand: Lives of significant individuals (and comparing across periods)
Local history: significant events, people and places in the locality
strands.gapNote: No app supports local-history enquiry; the famous-people app is national/international, not local.

suggestedApps.title: Our Place Then and Now
suggestedApps.concept: Drop a class photo of a local landmark next to a historic one; a slider wipes between them and pupils tap features (gas lamps, cobbles, a vanished mill) that show how the locality has changed.

strands.strand: Disciplinary knowledge: using simple sources and how we find out about the past
strands.gapNote: Sorting old/new objects touches artefacts but does not ask 'how do we know?' using photos, diaries or buildings as evidence.

suggestedApps.title: History Detective: Clues from the Past
suggestedApps.concept: An old object, a photo and a (simplified) diary line appear; children tap each source and answer 'what does this tell us?', building a class wall of evidence about one event.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: British prehistory to 1066 (Stone Age to Iron Age; Romans; Anglo-Saxons & Scots; Vikings)
strands.gapNote: No app covers the four mandatory British history units; the civilisation comparator is ancient world, not British chronology.

suggestedApps.title: Britain Through the Ages: Invaders Map
suggestedApps.concept: A morphing map of Britain where pupils slide through Stone Age, Roman, Anglo-Saxon and Viking layers, tapping settlements, hill forts, Hadrian's Wall and the Danelaw to see who arrived and what they left behind.

suggestedApps.title: 1066 Decision Room
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils weigh the rival claims of Harold, William and Hardrada with evidence cards, then watch a branching map of Stamford Bridge and Hastings unfold from their reasoning.

strands.strand: Earliest civilisations and Ancient Greece (Sumer/Indus/Egypt/Shang; Greek life and legacy)
A non-European society (early Islamic Baghdad, Maya, or Benin c.AD 900)
strands.gapNote: The comparator may include non-European civilisations but there is no dedicated Baghdad/Maya/Benin depth study; pitched as comparison not depth.

suggestedApps.title: House of Wisdom: Baghdad c.900
suggestedApps.concept: Walk a 3D-ish circular city; pupils enter the library, observatory and bazaar, each unlocking an interactive on Islamic advances in maths, astronomy and medicine and how knowledge travelled.

strands.strand: A study beyond 1066 (turning point/theme in British history, e.g. WWII, railways, migration)
strands.gapNote: No app addresses a post-1066 British theme/turning point.

suggestedApps.title: Turning Point Investigator
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils pick a candidate turning point (arrival of railways, Battle of Britain) and drag 'before' and 'after' images of daily life onto a balance to judge how much really changed.

strands.strand: Chronological understanding: securing the big picture across periods
strands.gapNote: Timeline Builder is pitched KS3 and abstract; KS2 needs a scaled period-by-period 'big picture' ribbon (BC/AD, overlapping civilisations).

suggestedApps.title: The Big Picture Timeline
suggestedApps.concept: A proportionally scaled BC/AD ribbon where pupils place studied periods (Egypt, Romans, Vikings) and see at a glance which overlapped and how long ago each was.

strands.strand: Disciplinary knowledge: historical enquiry and using/evaluating sources
Second-order concepts: cause, change, similarity/difference, significance
strands.gapNote: Comparison covers similarity/difference but causation and significance for KS2 are not addressed at this phase (existing causation apps are KS3/KS4).

suggestedApps.title: Why Did It Happen? Cause Cards
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils drag picture-cause cards (e.g. why the Romans invaded) into 'big reason / small reason' rings and rank them, with a class vote revealing the spread of opinion.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Medieval Britain 1066-1509: Church, state and society
strands.gapNote: No app covers medieval substantive content (Norman Conquest, Magna Carta, Black Death, feudalism).

suggestedApps.title: The Feudal Pyramid Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils assemble the feudal hierarchy by dragging king, barons, knights and peasants into place; pulling out a layer animates the obligations and consequences that ripple up and down.

suggestedApps.title: Black Death: Outbreak Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: A 1348 map where pupils set trade routes and watch plague spread, then test responses; live counters show population and economic collapse driving the Peasants' Revolt.

strands.strand: Early Modern Britain 1509-1745: Reformation, civil war, revolution
strands.gapNote: No app covers the Reformation, English Civil War or the constitutional settlement.

suggestedApps.title: Break with Rome: Henry VIII's Dilemma
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils advise Henry by weighing money, heirs, faith and power; each choice animates the dissolution of the monasteries and shifts a national 'Catholic-Protestant' meter.

suggestedApps.title: Civil War Allegiances Map
suggestedApps.concept: Colour England by Royalist/Parliamentarian using evidence cards on religion, region and class, then run the campaign to see how allegiance shaped the outcome.

strands.strand: Britain 1745-1901: ideas, political power, industry and empire (incl. slavery and abolition)
strands.gapNote: No app covers industrialisation, empire, the transatlantic slave trade/abolition or franchise reform.

suggestedApps.title: Industrial Town Machine
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils drag mills, canals, railways and workers onto a growing town and watch population, pollution and child-labour dials move, then enact reforms to see the social impact.

suggestedApps.title: Triangular Trade Decoded
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive Atlantic map traces the three legs of the slave trade; pupils click ports to surface testimony and abolition arguments, weighing what finally ended it.

strands.strand: Britain, Europe and the wider world 1901-present, including the mandatory Holocaust study
strands.gapNote: Propaganda decoder (tagged KS4) touches 20th-century material but there is no Holocaust study or WWI/WWII/welfare-state content at KS3.

suggestedApps.title: Voices of the Holocaust: Why It Matters
suggestedApps.concept: A respectful, age-appropriate timeline of persecution to liberation, where pupils unlock survivor testimony and reflect on choices, resistance and remembrance (no graphic imagery).

suggestedApps.title: Total War Home Front
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils run a WWII household managing rationing, evacuation and the Blitz, with decisions feeding into a class picture of how war changed everyday British life.

strands.strand: Local history study and pre-1066 consolidation (migration, changing power/landscape over time)
strands.gapNote: No app supports a local-history enquiry or a long-arc 'theme over time' consolidation.

suggestedApps.title: Migration to Britain: The Long View
suggestedApps.concept: A single map animated across 2,000 years; pupils tap waves of arrivals (Romans, Saxons, Huguenots, Windrush) and chart continuity and change in who came and why.

strands.strand: World history study and its interconnections (e.g. Mughal India, Qing China, Russian empires)
strands.gapNote: No app covers a non-British world-history society at KS3.

suggestedApps.title: Empires Connected
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils explore a chosen world empire (Mughal India or Qing China) and draw trade, idea and conflict links to Europe, revealing global interconnection rather than isolated history.

strands.strand: Disciplinary concepts: chronology, causation, change/continuity, significance
Disciplinary concepts: source evaluation, interpretation and historical perspective/empathy

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (GCSE; DfE subject content DFE-00346-2014, Ofqual-regulated; AQA/Edexcel/OCR/Eduqas)

strands.strand: Period study (50+ years of national/international history, e.g. Germany 1890-1945, American West)
strands.gapNote: No app teaches a substantive GCSE period study; existing apps are concept tools only.

suggestedApps.title: Weimar to Nazi Germany: Power Tracker
suggestedApps.concept: Students slide through 1918-1934 watching economy, votes and street violence interact; each pause unlocks the key event and lets them test 'how did Hitler come to power?' causes.

suggestedApps.title: American West Frontier Sim
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive plains map where students balance settlers, railroads, cattle, law and Plains Nations to see change and conflict over c1835-1895.

strands.strand: Thematic study across centuries (Health/Medicine, Power and the People, Crime and Punishment, Migration)
strands.gapNote: No app traces a long thematic development (a core GCSE element worth 30-40%).

suggestedApps.title: Medicine Through Time Timeline
suggestedApps.concept: A scrollable c1250-present timeline where students drag breakthroughs (Galen, germ theory, NHS) into place and a 'factor lens' highlights what drove or held back progress.

suggestedApps.title: Power and the People: Protest Ladder
suggestedApps.concept: Students plot Magna Carta, Peasants' Revolt, Chartists and suffrage on a rights ladder, judging turning points and continuity in the struggle for power.

strands.strand: British depth study with historic environment (Norman/Elizabethan/Restoration England + linked site)
strands.gapNote: Source tools support the skills but no app delivers the substantive depth content or the historic-environment site enquiry.

suggestedApps.title: Elizabethan England Investigator
suggestedApps.concept: Explore court, religion and the Armada through interactive panels, then 'visit' a linked historic site and reason about what its physical features reveal.

strands.strand: Non-British depth study (conflict/crisis, e.g. Cold War, Russia/USSR, USA Civil Rights)
strands.gapNote: Revolution-vs-Rebellion touches conflict comparison but does not teach a specified non-British depth period in detail.

suggestedApps.title: Cold War Flashpoints
suggestedApps.concept: A world map of Berlin, Cuba and Vietnam; students step through each crisis, choosing superpower responses and watching a tension meter to grasp escalation and detente.

strands.strand: Source analysis and evaluation (AO3: provenance, utility, reliability)
Interpretations: how and why historians/accounts differ (AO4)
Second-order concepts and significance (AO1/AO2: cause, change, significance, judgement)

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (A-level; DfE GCE subject content 2014, Ofqual-regulated; AQA/Edexcel/OCR)

strands.strand: Breadth study: change and continuity over c.100+ years (Component 1)
strands.gapNote: Change/Continuity Analyser (tagged KS3) is conceptually relevant but not pitched to A-level long-arc breadth analysis or a specified breadth topic.

suggestedApps.title: Breadth Study Change Mapper
suggestedApps.concept: Students plot political, economic, social and religious threads across a 100-year breadth topic on parallel lanes, then test periodisation by sliding turning-point markers and justifying them.

strands.strand: Depth study: causal complexity and contingency over 25-50 years (Component 2)
strands.gapNote: Causation tools (KS3/KS4-tagged) help but there is no A-level depth tool handling contingency, counterfactual reasoning or layered causation.

suggestedApps.title: Contingency and Counterfactual Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Students remove or alter a single factor in a depth-study crisis and the app reasons out how the chain of events might have changed, surfacing the difference between underlying and trigger causes.

strands.strand: Primary source evaluation in context (AO2: provenance, value, utility)
strands.gapNote: GCSE/KS3-pitched source tools exist but nothing models A-level provenance interrogation (NOP) and purpose-dependent value judgement at the required rigour.

suggestedApps.title: Source Value NOP Workbench
suggestedApps.concept: Students dissect a primary source by Nature, Origin and Purpose, then watch how its assessed value shifts as the enquiry question changes, building substantiated AO2 judgements.

strands.strand: Historiography and interpretations (AO3: evaluating historians' arguments)
Extended analytical essay writing and substantiated argument (AO1)
Non-Examined Assessment: independent historical investigation
strands.gapNote: No app scaffolds the NEA (question formulation, 100-year context, managing sources and historiography across ~4,000 words).

suggestedApps.title: NEA Question Forge
suggestedApps.concept: Students draft an investigation question; the app stress-tests it for debate, source availability, 100-year context and 'to what extent' framing, then builds a research and word-budget plan.

summary.headline: Disciplinary skills (sources, causation, interpretation, significance) are well covered across KS3-KS5, but substantive period content is almost entirely missing: KS2 British history (Stone Age to 1066), the whole of KS3 British and world history, and the GCSE/A-level period, thematic and depth studies have no apps at all.

subject: Geography
sourceNotes: Curriculum strands extracted from local knowledge files in knowledge/geography-curriculum/01_national_curriculum (eyfs.md, ks1.md, ks2.md, ks3.md, ks4.md GCSE, ks5.md A-level). App inventory from /tmp/subject_apps.json (20 Geography apps). Coverage judged against app name + description only.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (within Understanding the World: People, Culture and Communities; The Natural World)

strands.strand: Describing the immediate environment and simple maps
Weather, seasons and changes in the natural world
Similarities and differences between this country and other places/communities
strands.gapNote: No app compares life here with life in contrasting countries or cultural communities.

suggestedApps.title: Around the World Storybox
suggestedApps.concept: Tap two homes on a globe (e.g. a UK street and a contrasting country) and a split-screen shows children, homes, food and weather to drag-match same/different.

strands.strand: Exploring the natural world (plants, animals, contrasting environments)
