strands.gapNote: Weather is touched but plants, animals and contrasting habitats are not interactively explored at EYFS level.

suggestedApps.title: Habitat Explorer Garden
suggestedApps.concept: Drag animals and plants into the right environment (pond, woodland, hot place, cold place) and watch them thrive or not, with read-aloud labels.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Locational knowledge: continents, oceans, UK countries and capitals
strands.gapNote: Continents and oceans covered; UK four countries and capital cities not explicitly an app.

suggestedApps.title: UK Countries and Capitals
suggestedApps.concept: Drag England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland onto a UK outline, then pin each capital city and hear a fact pop up.

strands.strand: Place knowledge: comparing a UK area with a contrasting non-European area
strands.gapNote: No app compares a small UK locality with a contrasting non-European locality.

suggestedApps.title: Here and There Compare
suggestedApps.concept: Side-by-side photo-map of a UK village and a contrasting locality; pupils drag features into a same/different Venn diagram.

strands.strand: Human and physical geography: weather patterns, hot/cold areas, basic vocabulary
strands.gapNote: Seaside app teaches human/physical features but hot/cold areas relative to Equator and Poles is not addressed.

suggestedApps.title: Equator Hot and Cold
suggestedApps.concept: Spin a globe with the Equator and Poles lit up; drag a thermometer to any spot to reveal hot, temperate or cold and matching scenery.

strands.strand: Geographical skills and fieldwork: maps, compass directions, aerial photos, fieldwork
strands.gapNote: Fieldwork tallying is covered; simple N/S/E/W compass work and aerial-to-plan recognition pitched at KS1 is missing (map-skills app is KS2 level).

suggestedApps.title: Bird's Eye School
suggestedApps.concept: Flip between a ground photo and the aerial view of a school, then build a simple plan map using picture symbols and a key.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Locational knowledge: UK counties/cities/regions, Europe, North and South America
strands.gapNote: No app builds locational knowledge of UK counties/cities or maps Europe and the Americas.

suggestedApps.title: Map the Americas
suggestedApps.concept: Drag countries, capitals and physical features (Andes, Amazon, Rockies) onto blank continent maps with instant accuracy feedback.

suggestedApps.title: UK Counties and Cities Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Assemble a UK map county-by-county, drop in major cities and rivers, and reveal a regional fact card for each.

strands.strand: Latitude, longitude, hemispheres, tropics, time zones
strands.gapNote: No app covers the global grid (Equator, tropics, Greenwich Meridian, time zones).

suggestedApps.title: Lines on the Globe
suggestedApps.concept: Rotate a 3D Earth; toggle Equator, tropics and Prime Meridian on, then drag a clock to any longitude to see local time change by 15 degrees per hour.

strands.strand: Place knowledge: comparative region study (UK, Europe, Americas)
strands.gapNote: Biomes explorer gives regional environment data but not a structured side-by-side region comparison.

suggestedApps.title: Region Compare Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Pick a UK region and an overseas region; sliders reveal climate, land use and population side by side for guided comparison.

strands.strand: Physical geography: climate zones, biomes and vegetation belts
Physical geography: rivers, mountains, volcanoes, earthquakes and the water cycle
strands.gapNote: Strong KS3-pitched apps exist for water cycle, rivers and tectonics but none simplified to KS2 level; mountains as landforms not covered.

suggestedApps.title: Volcano Cross-Section
suggestedApps.concept: Click layers of a volcano to see magma rise and erupt, then label the parts in a drag-drop diagram at primary reading level.

strands.strand: Human geography: settlements, land use, economic activity, trade and resources
strands.gapNote: No app addresses settlements, land use, trade or resource distribution at KS2.

suggestedApps.title: Build a Settlement
suggestedApps.concept: Place houses, shops, farms and a river crossing on a landscape and watch the model explain why settlements grow where they do.

suggestedApps.title: Where Does It Come From?
suggestedApps.concept: Drag everyday goods (chocolate, bananas, trainers) onto a world map to trace their origins and simple trade routes.

strands.strand: Geographical skills and fieldwork: OS maps, grid references, compass, fieldwork

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Locational knowledge: globally significant places, terrestrial and marine
strands.gapNote: No app builds a mental map of globally significant places, oceans and marine features at KS3.

suggestedApps.title: World in Layers
suggestedApps.concept: Toggle thematic overlays (relief, climate, oceans, cities) on a world map to see how physical and human patterns line up.

strands.strand: Physical geography: tectonics, weathering, erosion and landforms
Physical geography: weather, climate and climate change
Ecosystems, biomes and human impacts (deforestation, desertification)
strands.gapNote: Climate graphs touch biome classification but ecosystem structure and human impacts are not interactive.

suggestedApps.title: Rainforest Under Pressure
suggestedApps.concept: Adjust logging, farming and protection sliders on a rainforest and watch biodiversity, carbon and indigenous land respond over time.

strands.strand: Human geography: population, migration and urbanisation
strands.gapNote: Population and urban structure apps exist but are tagged KS4; migration flows not covered at KS3.

suggestedApps.title: Migration Flows Map
suggestedApps.concept: Drag push and pull factors onto source and destination countries and watch animated migration arrows and impacts update.

strands.strand: Human geography: economic activity, development and globalisation
strands.gapNote: Development indicators app exists but is KS4-pitched; globalisation and economic sectors not covered.

suggestedApps.title: Globalisation Web
suggestedApps.concept: Click a transnational company and watch its supply chain, factories and markets light up across a world map with trade-off cards.

strands.strand: Resource geography: energy, food and water security
strands.gapNote: No app addresses resource distribution, security or the energy mix.

suggestedApps.title: Energy Mix Balancer
suggestedApps.concept: Slide a country's energy sources to meet demand while watching cost, emissions and security gauges respond.

strands.strand: Geographical skills and fieldwork: GIS, OS maps, data analysis, enquiry
strands.gapNote: No app teaches OS map interpretation, GIS or the enquiry/data-handling cycle at KS3.

suggestedApps.title: OS Map Detective
suggestedApps.concept: Interpret a live OS map: solve grid-reference, contour and distance challenges with instant marking and a relief profile tool.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (GCSE; DfE subject content DFE-00345-2014)

strands.strand: Natural hazards: tectonic and weather hazards
strands.gapNote: Tectonic hazards strongly covered; tropical storms as a weather hazard only indirectly via fronts app.

suggestedApps.title: Tropical Storm Tracker
suggestedApps.concept: Steer a developing tropical storm across warm ocean and land, watching wind, pressure and storm surge change and impacts accumulate.

strands.strand: The living world: ecosystems, rainforests and cold/desert environments
strands.gapNote: Biome climate covered but rainforest deforestation and cold/desert environment management not modelled at GCSE depth.

suggestedApps.title: Ecosystem Nutrient Cycle
suggestedApps.concept: Drag biomass, litter and soil stores in a rainforest nutrient cycle and see deforestation break the loop.

strands.strand: UK physical landscapes: coasts, rivers and glaciation
Urban issues and challenges (HICs and LICs/NEEs)
strands.gapNote: Urban land-use models covered; rapid LIC/NEE urban growth and sustainable city strategies less directly addressed.

suggestedApps.title: Sustainable City Planner
suggestedApps.concept: Balance housing, transport, green space and energy in a growing city; live dashboards score liveability and emissions.

strands.strand: The changing economic world: development gap and TNCs
Resource management: food, water and energy
strands.gapNote: No app covers resource supply, demand, insecurity or management at GCSE.

suggestedApps.title: Water Security Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: Manage a region's water supply against demand, drought and transfer schemes, watching scarcity and conflict indices respond.

strands.strand: Geographical skills and fieldwork enquiry (cartographic, graphical, statistical)
strands.gapNote: Graph building and scatter plots present, but OS map skills, statistical techniques and the fieldwork enquiry cycle are not directly taught.

suggestedApps.title: Fieldwork Enquiry Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Walk through a coastal or urban enquiry: choose sampling, plot collected data and evaluate reliability with guided prompts.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (A-level; DfE subject content December 2014)

strands.strand: Water and carbon cycles (systems)
Coastal systems and landscapes
strands.gapNote: Coastal landforms covered; sediment-cell systems framing pitched at A-level less explicit.

suggestedApps.title: Sediment Cell Modeller
suggestedApps.concept: Adjust sources, transfers and sinks in a coastal sediment cell and watch the budget and landforms respond to management.

strands.strand: Optional physical: glaciers, hot deserts, ecosystems under stress or hazards
strands.gapNote: Glaciation and hazards touched but not at A-level systems depth (e.g. hazard risk equation, glacial mass balance).

suggestedApps.title: Glacier Mass Balance
suggestedApps.concept: Adjust accumulation and ablation over years to model glacier advance/retreat and equilibrium line altitude shifts.

strands.strand: Global systems and global governance
Changing places
strands.gapNote: No app addresses place identity, representation, meaning and lived experience.

suggestedApps.title: Place Profiler
suggestedApps.concept: Layer demographic, economic and qualitative data plus media representations onto a place to compare insider and outsider perspectives.

strands.strand: Optional human: contemporary urban, population/environment or resource security
strands.gapNote: Urban models and population structure exist but are GCSE-pitched; resource security and population-environment relationships not modelled at A-level.

suggestedApps.title: Population and Resources Modeller
suggestedApps.concept: Run Malthus vs Boserup scenarios by adjusting population, technology and resources and watch carrying-capacity outcomes diverge.

strands.strand: Geographical and quantitative skills and the independent investigation (NEA)
strands.gapNote: Some data visualisation present but A-level statistical tests (Spearman, chi-square, significance) and the enquiry process are not covered.

suggestedApps.title: Stats Test Chooser
suggestedApps.concept: Feed in a sample dataset and the tool walks through selecting and running Spearman's rank or chi-square, interpreting significance live.

summary.headline: Physical geography (tectonics, rivers, coasts, glaciation, water/carbon cycles) is very well covered, but human geography (settlements, migration, development, resource management) and skills/fieldwork plus locational knowledge are the biggest gaps, with resource management uncovered at every secondary phase.

subject: Religious Education
sourceNotes: Primary source: local knowledge files at knowledge/rs-curriculum/01_national_curriculum (eyfs.md, ks1.md, ks2.md, ks3.md, ks4.md, ks5.md, overview.md). RE is statutory but sits outside the National Curriculum: content is set locally by SACREs via locally agreed syllabuses, benchmarked against the REC National Content Standard 2023, with GCSE (DfE 2016 subject content) and A-level (DfE 2015 subject content) at KS4/KS5. Existing apps read from /tmp/subject_apps.json key "RE" (20 apps).

keyStages.statutoryNote: non-statutory (RE foundations within the People, Culture and Communities ELG; systematic RE begins at KS1)

strands.strand: Special times and celebrations across families and communities
Special places of worship
Special stories and special people from religious traditions
strands.gapNote: Only a KS1 stories app exists; nothing pitched at Reception with picture-book story exploration.

suggestedApps.title: Story Carpet
suggestedApps.concept: Tap-through animated picture-book stories from each tradition with simple 'what happened next' sequencing for the youngest children.

strands.strand: Special objects, symbols and artefacts (sensory exploration)
strands.gapNote: No EYFS app for handling and naming religious artefacts.

suggestedApps.title: Mystery Artefact Box
suggestedApps.concept: Drag a sensory 'feely box' over screen artefacts that zoom and reveal what they are and which community uses them.

strands.strand: Belonging: families, communities and respect for diversity
strands.gapNote: Belonging is only touched at KS1; no EYFS-level 'we all belong' activity celebrating diversity.

suggestedApps.title: All Are Welcome
suggestedApps.concept: Build a class picture by dragging diverse families, foods and celebrations into a shared community scene that lights up as it fills.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (basic curriculum; content set by locally agreed syllabus, not the National Curriculum)

strands.strand: Special stories from sacred texts and oral tradition
Signs, symbols, artefacts and belonging
Festivals and special times (Christmas, Easter, Eid, Diwali, Hanukkah)
strands.gapNote: Only an EYFS celebrations app exists; nothing KS1-specific linking festivals to their stories and meanings.

suggestedApps.title: Festival Calendar Wheel
suggestedApps.concept: Spin a year wheel to each festival, then match the story, food and symbol that belong to it across Christianity, Islam, Judaism and Hinduism.

strands.strand: Special people and leaders (Jesus, Muhammad, Guru Nanak, the Buddha)
strands.gapNote: No app introducing key founders and exemplars and what they taught.

suggestedApps.title: Who Taught This?
suggestedApps.concept: Match a simple teaching or kind action to the religious leader who is known for it, with a short illustrated story reveal.

strands.strand: Special places of worship and what makes them special
strands.gapNote: Covered at EYFS only; a KS1 app would go deeper into named features and their uses.

suggestedApps.title: Inside the Place of Worship
suggestedApps.concept: Pan around 360-degree church, mosque, mandir and synagogue interiors, tapping hotspots that name each feature and explain its purpose.

strands.strand: Big questions and learning from religion (meaning, right and wrong)
strands.gapNote: No KS1 app prompting age-appropriate reflection on 'big questions'.

suggestedApps.title: Wonder Wall
suggestedApps.concept: Children post and sort 'I wonder...' questions onto a class wall, then explore how different traditions respond.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (basic curriculum; content set by locally agreed syllabus, not the National Curriculum)

strands.strand: Beliefs about God, creation and the human condition across traditions
strands.gapNote: Creation app is pitched at KS3; no KS2-level app comparing core beliefs about God and creation.

suggestedApps.title: What Do They Believe About God?
suggestedApps.concept: Slide between traditions to see child-friendly statements about God (one, many forms, no creator) with linked stories and symbols.

strands.strand: Sacred texts: structure, authority and interpretation
strands.gapNote: Sacred Texts Comparator is a KS3 analysis tool; KS2 needs a simpler 'how is this book used and treated' explorer.

suggestedApps.title: Handle With Reverence
suggestedApps.concept: Explore how each holy book (Bible, Qur'an, Torah, Guru Granth Sahib) is stored, carried and read, dragging the right care-practice to each text.

strands.strand: Worship, prayer and ritual
Rites of passage and life-cycle ceremonies
Festivals, pilgrimage and the religious year
strands.gapNote: Pilgrimage Explorer is KS3-pitched; no KS2 app linking festivals and pilgrimage to their meanings for believers.

suggestedApps.title: Journey and Festival Map
suggestedApps.concept: Click pilgrimage destinations and festival markers on a world map to unlock why each matters and what believers do there.

strands.strand: Religious values, ethics and faith in action (justice, charity, ahimsa, seva)
strands.gapNote: No KS2 app exploring how beliefs shape values and charitable action.

suggestedApps.title: Faith in Action
suggestedApps.concept: Match real charitable acts to the value and tradition that inspires them (zakah, tzedakah, seva), building a 'kindness in the world' board.

strands.strand: Non-religious worldviews (Humanism) and ultimate questions
strands.gapNote: No KS2 app introducing Humanist responses to big questions alongside religious ones.

suggestedApps.title: Big Question Sorter
suggestedApps.concept: Pose questions like 'why are we here?' and sort religious and Humanist answers side by side onto a comparison board.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (basic curriculum; content set by locally agreed syllabus, benchmarked against the REC National Content Standard 2023)

strands.strand: Beliefs, teachings and sources of authority across the six principal religions
Worship, ritual, symbolism and religious expression
Pilgrimage, festivals and sacred places
Diversity within traditions (denominations, schools, Sunni/Shia, lived religion)
strands.gapNote: Venn compares whole worldviews but does not surface internal diversity (e.g. denominations, madhhabs, Orthodox/Reform).

suggestedApps.title: One Religion, Many Branches
suggestedApps.concept: Expand a branching tree for each religion (Catholic/Orthodox/Protestant, Sunni/Shia/Sufi) to see where beliefs and practices diverge and why.

strands.strand: Non-religious worldviews: Humanism, atheism, agnosticism, secular ethics
strands.gapNote: Worldview Venn can include non-religious views but there is no dedicated app teaching Humanism/atheism/agnosticism in their own terms.

suggestedApps.title: The Non-Religious Map
suggestedApps.concept: Position yourself on sliders (belief in God, source of morality, life after death) and see where Humanist, atheist and agnostic positions sit.

strands.strand: Ethical reasoning: applying religious and non-religious frameworks to issues
strands.gapNote: Ethics Dilemma is pitched at KS4; KS3 needs an introductory framework-comparison tool on contemporary issues.

suggestedApps.title: Big Questions Debate Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Pick an issue (environment, animal rights), pull in religious and Humanist viewpoints, and assemble a balanced two-sided argument on screen.

strands.strand: Religious identity, belonging and religion in contemporary Britain
strands.gapNote: No app on lived religion, multiple identities, or 'believing without belonging' in modern Britain.

suggestedApps.title: Identity Mosaic
suggestedApps.concept: Build composite British identities by layering faith, ethnicity, culture and practice, revealing how identity is complex and individual.

keyStages.statutoryNote: dual: statutory Core RE for all pupils (locally agreed syllabus) plus exam-board-defined GCSE Religious Studies (DfE 2016 subject content; AQA/Edexcel/OCR)

strands.strand: Study of two religions: beliefs, teachings and practices (GCSE Component 1)
strands.gapNote: KS3 comparator apps give breadth but nothing supports the GCSE in-depth, exam-keyed study of two chosen religions including common/divergent views.

suggestedApps.title: GCSE Beliefs Revision Engine
suggestedApps.concept: Select your two GCSE religions and drill into beliefs, teachings and practices with quote-evidence cards and common-vs-divergent view toggles.

strands.strand: Thematic studies: relationships, religion and life, peace and conflict, crime and punishment, human rights (GCSE Component 2)
strands.gapNote: Rights/responsibilities and general dilemmas touch some themes; no coverage of relationships/families, peace and conflict, or crime and punishment with religious teachings.

suggestedApps.title: Theme Explorer: Peace and Conflict
suggestedApps.concept: Work a conflict scenario through just war, pacifism and forgiveness teachings, dragging scripture and non-religious views onto each position.

suggestedApps.title: Crime and Punishment Balancer
suggestedApps.concept: Adjust aims of punishment (retribution, deterrence, reformation) and see how religious and secular thinkers would judge a case.

strands.strand: Existence of God and revelation (arguments and their critiques)
strands.gapNote: Argument Mapper builds structures but is not anchored to the design/First Cause/revelation arguments and counter-arguments at GCSE level.

suggestedApps.title: Does God Exist? Argument Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Construct the design and First Cause arguments brick by brick, then fire counter-arguments at them and judge whether they still stand.

strands.strand: Ethical theory and moral decision-making
Beliefs about life, death and the afterlife
Evaluative writing and constructing reasoned arguments (AO2 / 12-mark technique)
strands.gapNote: Argument Mapper helps structure logic but does not model GCSE-style evaluative essay writing against the mark scheme.

suggestedApps.title: 12-Mark Essay Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Drag argument, counter-argument, scripture and judgement blocks into an evaluative paragraph that scores live against the GCSE AO2 descriptors.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board-defined A-level Religious Studies (DfE 2015 subject content; AQA/OCR/Eduqas); statutory entitlement to collective worship continues but RE is otherwise optional post-16

strands.strand: Philosophy of religion: arguments for God's existence and their critiques
The problem of evil and theodicies
strands.gapNote: No app on the logical/evidential problem of evil, soul-making, free-will defence or process theodicy.

suggestedApps.title: Theodicy Tester
suggestedApps.concept: Throw natural and moral evil cases at each theodicy (Hick, Plantinga, process) and watch which claims survive and where they break down.

strands.strand: Religious experience and religious language
strands.gapNote: No app on typologies of experience, Swinburne's principles, or the verification/falsification and symbol/analogy debates.

suggestedApps.title: Is This Experience Veridical?
suggestedApps.concept: Apply credulity and testimony principles to case studies of numinous and mystical experiences and weigh naturalistic counter-explanations.

suggestedApps.title: Meaningful or Meaningless?
suggestedApps.concept: Run religious statements through verification, falsification, bliks, symbol and analogy lenses to test whether they carry cognitive meaning.

strands.strand: Normative ethical theories (natural law, situation ethics, virtue ethics, Kant, utilitarianism)
strands.gapNote: Medical Ethics and trolley apps apply frameworks but there is no dedicated tool teaching and contrasting the normative theories themselves at A-level depth.

suggestedApps.title: Ethical Theory Workbench
suggestedApps.concept: Feed one dilemma to natural law, situation ethics, virtue ethics, Kant and Bentham in parallel and compare their reasoning and verdicts side by side.

strands.strand: Applied ethics: life and death, animal ethics, bioethics
Meta-ethics, conscience and free will / moral responsibility
strands.gapNote: No app on naturalism/intuitionism/divine command, the Euthyphro dilemma, conscience, or determinism vs libertarianism.

suggestedApps.title: Euthyphro and Beyond
suggestedApps.concept: Navigate a branching debate on whether good is good because God commands it, then map naturalist, intuitionist and emotivist positions on a meta-ethics grid.

suggestedApps.title: Free Will Courtroom
suggestedApps.concept: Try a defendant under libertarian, hard-determinist and compatibilist assumptions and decide whether moral responsibility holds in each.

strands.strand: Study of religion: sacred texts, developments in thought, religion and society/dialogue
strands.gapNote: No app on New Testament/textual study, historical developments, secularism/pluralism, or exclusivism/inclusivism/pluralism models.

suggestedApps.title: Pluralism Positions
suggestedApps.concept: Place religions on an exclusivist-to-pluralist spectrum and stress-test each model against real interfaith scenarios.

suggestedApps.title: Reading the Text Critically
suggestedApps.concept: Apply source, form and redaction criticism to a Gospel passage, toggling historical, literary and theological lenses on the same verses.

summary.headline: Core comparative content and applied ethics are well covered across KS3 to KS5, but the biggest gaps are GCSE-specific exam preparation at KS4 (thematic studies, two-religion depth, evaluative writing) and large parts of A-level philosophy (problem of evil, religious language/experience, meta-ethics) alongside thin primary coverage of religious values, leaders and non-religious worldviews.

subject: Modern Foreign Languages
sourceNotes: Primary source: local NC summaries in knowledge/mfl-curriculum/01_national_curriculum (overview.md, eyfs.md, ks1.md, ks2.md, ks3.md, ks4.md, ks5.md). Strands framed around the four skills (listening, speaking, reading, writing), the Ofsted 2021 three pillars (phonics, vocabulary, grammar) and intercultural understanding, plus exam-board GCSE/A-level structures. App inventory from /tmp/subject_apps.json key "MFL" (20 apps).

keyStages.statutoryNote: non-statutory (MFL not in EYFS framework; sits within Communication and Language / Understanding the World; early language awareness only)

strands.strand: Early language awareness and listening to sounds of other languages
Speaking and joining in: greetings, songs and rhymes in another language
Early number and core vocabulary through play
Curiosity about other cultures and celebrating linguistic diversity
strands.gapNote: Hello World shows flags but there is no app exploring festivals, food or daily life of other cultures at EYFS level.

suggestedApps.title: Around the World Story Carpet
suggestedApps.concept: A projected world map where tapping a country plays a short greeting, a flag, and a culture snapshot (a festival, a food, an animal) so children explore diversity by pointing and listening together.

keyStages.statutoryNote: non-statutory (MFL not statutory until KS2; early exposure to vocabulary and culture only)

strands.strand: Core vocabulary acquisition (colours, animals, everyday words)
Listening for meaning and matching sound to picture
Speaking and repeating familiar words with accurate pronunciation
strands.gapNote: Vocabulary apps present words but neither focuses on modelling and rehearsing pronunciation (say-after-me, record-and-compare) at KS1 level.

suggestedApps.title: Say It Like a Native
suggestedApps.concept: Children tap a picture, hear a native speaker, then echo it; a friendly sound-meter shows how closely the rhythm matched, encouraging whole-class call-and-response.

strands.strand: Songs, rhymes and number patterns in the target language
strands.gapNote: No KS1 app uses songs/rhymes, the most powerful primary tool for internalising rhythm and intonation.

suggestedApps.title: Action Song Builder
suggestedApps.concept: A projected karaoke-style song (counting or greetings) with highlighted words and matching actions the class performs, with a slider to slow the tempo for new learners.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (Foreign Language, since 2014; modern or ancient language)

strands.strand: Listening: understand spoken language and respond
strands.gapNote: Classroom Phrases involves some listening but there is no dedicated KS2 listening-comprehension app for gist and detail in short passages.

suggestedApps.title: Listen and Show
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils hear a short spoken description (a person, a room, the weather) and drag items onto a scene to show understanding, with replay and an embedded transcript reveal.

strands.strand: Speaking: conversations, asking and answering questions, presenting orally
strands.gapNote: Weather Reporter supports a presentation task and Classroom Phrases gives stock phrases, but two-way conversation/question-answer practice is thin.

suggestedApps.title: Two-Way Talk Cards
suggestedApps.concept: A flip-card dialogue mat where the teacher and class take turns: tap a question card, a model answer appears, then pupils substitute their own details to rebuild the exchange.

strands.strand: Phonics: link spelling, sound and meaning of words
strands.gapNote: Pronunciation Rules Guide covers sound-spelling but is pitched at KS3; no KS2-friendly phonics introduction.

suggestedApps.title: Sound Spotter
suggestedApps.concept: A projected grid of target-language graphemes; tap one to hear it and see three example words, then a class game where pupils sort picture-words by their hidden sound.

strands.strand: Reading: understand words, phrases and simple writing; use a dictionary
strands.gapNote: No KS2 reading-comprehension app; nothing teaches reading short texts or using a bilingual dictionary.

suggestedApps.title: Read and Match
suggestedApps.concept: Short illustrated sentences pupils match to pictures, with a tap-to-reveal mini dictionary so they practise looking up unknown words in context.

strands.strand: Writing: write phrases from memory and adapt them; describe people, places and things
strands.gapNote: Weather Reporter uses sentence building for one topic; broader adapt-a-phrase writing across topics is not covered at KS2.

suggestedApps.title: Phrase Remix
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils start from a model sentence and swap colour-coded slots (noun, adjective, place) to write new descriptions, building a class gallery of self-made sentences.

strands.strand: Basic grammar: gender, articles and high-frequency verb conjugation
strands.gapNote: Gender Patterns and Sentence Builder exist but are pitched at KS3; no gentle KS2 introduction to gender and simple verbs.

suggestedApps.title: Le, La, Les Sorting Game
suggestedApps.concept: Drag nouns into the correct article basket with instant feedback and a hint that reveals the ending pattern, scaling difficulty as the class succeeds.

strands.strand: Intercultural understanding and appreciation of stories/songs
strands.gapNote: No KS2 app explores culture, festivals or authentic stories in the target language.

suggestedApps.title: A Day in the Life
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive timeline of a child's day in a French/Spanish/German town; tap moments to compare school, meals and routines with the UK, with key vocabulary surfacing in context.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (Modern Foreign Language, Years 7 to 9)

strands.strand: Tenses and verb manipulation (present, past, future; voices and moods)
Core grammar: gender, articles, adjective agreement, sentence structure and word order
Vocabulary building across topics and by frequency/level
Phonics: sound-spelling correspondence, pronunciation and intonation
Listening: obtain information from spoken language and transcribe
strands.gapNote: Sound Discrimination Trainer is KS4-pitched and tests phoneme contrasts, not gist/detail comprehension of passages at KS3.

suggestedApps.title: Catch the Detail
suggestedApps.concept: Authentic-style audio clips with a live transcription challenge: pupils type what they hear word by word, with adjustable speed and a reveal that highlights what they missed.

strands.strand: Speaking: initiate and develop conversations with increasing spontaneity
strands.gapNote: No KS3 app supports interactive spontaneous speaking, coping with unexpected responses or social conventions.

suggestedApps.title: Conversation Roulette
suggestedApps.concept: A spinner serves a random question and an unexpected follow-up; pupils must respond live using a scaffold of connectives, with the teacher escalating spontaneity as confidence grows.

strands.strand: Reading and translation of authentic and literary texts
strands.gapNote: No KS3 reading-comprehension or translation app; literary text reading for KS3 is unaddressed.

suggestedApps.title: Translate the Text
suggestedApps.concept: A short authentic passage where pupils drag English equivalents under each chunk, with a confidence heat-map showing which structures the class found hardest.

strands.strand: Writing: connected prose and creative expression
strands.gapNote: Sentence Builder helps at sentence level but there is no KS3 tool for building paragraphs/connected prose or creative writing.

suggestedApps.title: Paragraph Climber
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils assemble a short paragraph by linking sentences with connectives and opinion phrases, earning a complexity score that rewards subordination and tense variety.

strands.strand: Intercultural understanding of target-language countries
strands.gapNote: No KS3 app addresses culture, customs or authentic cultural materials.

suggestedApps.title: Culture Compare
suggestedApps.concept: Side-by-side interactive panels (festivals, school day, food, etiquette) for a target-language country versus the UK, with pupils predicting differences before revealing the answer.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (GCSE, AQA/Edexcel/OCR; non-statutory but EBacc pillar)

strands.strand: Grammar mastery for GCSE (tenses, cases, agreement) and defined grammar lists
Vocabulary breadth across GCSE themes and false-friend awareness
strands.gapNote: False Friends and the word planner help, but there is no app systematically drilling the ~1,200-1,500 word GCSE vocabulary lists by theme with spaced retrieval.

suggestedApps.title: Theme Vocab Sprint
suggestedApps.concept: A spaced-retrieval flashcard arena organised by the three GCSE themes; the class races a leaderboard while the algorithm resurfaces words pupils keep missing.

strands.strand: Listening comprehension and sound discrimination
Speaking: role play, photo card and general conversation
strands.gapNote: No app rehearses the three GCSE speaking tasks (role play with unpredictable element, photo card, general conversation).

suggestedApps.title: Photo Card Coach
suggestedApps.concept: Projects a GCSE-style photo card, generates likely questions, and runs a countdown for pupils to describe, give opinions and react spontaneously, then reveals model answer chunks.

suggestedApps.title: Role Play Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: A transactional scenario where one unpredictable prompt is hidden until revealed, training pupils to improvise within a role play structure.

strands.strand: Reading and bidirectional translation (TL to English and English to TL)
strands.gapNote: Translation into and out of the target language is mandatory at GCSE but no app practises it; False Friends only touches one pitfall.

suggestedApps.title: Translation Trainer
suggestedApps.concept: Sentences to translate both directions with a chunk-by-chunk marking scheme that flags lost tense, agreement or word-order points and scores like an examiner.

strands.strand: Writing: extended responses, sentence complexity and translation
Cultural and literature/film engagement at GCSE
strands.gapNote: No app supports the customs/festivals content or the optional literature/film responses at GCSE.

suggestedApps.title: Festival Explorer
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive deep-dive into a target-language festival or tradition with embedded authentic clips and opinion-building prompts pupils use to draft a writing response.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (A-level AQA/Edexcel/OCR and IB; no national curriculum applies)

strands.strand: Advanced grammar and sophisticated language manipulation
strands.gapNote: Existing grammar apps are KS4-pitched; there is no A-level tool for complex structures such as subjunctive, conditional perfect or advanced syntax.

suggestedApps.title: Subjunctive Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Trigger-phrase prompts where pupils decide if the subjunctive is required and conjugate it, with a live explanation panel justifying each mood choice.

strands.strand: Literary text analysis (theme, style, characterisation)
Essay writing: argument, advanced connectives and formal register
Listening and bidirectional translation at A-level standard
strands.gapNote: Sound discrimination is KS4 level; no app handles A-level listening passages or the demanding two-way translation in Paper 1.

suggestedApps.title: Advanced Translation Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Idiomatic A-level passages to render both directions, with a side panel surfacing register, idiom and structural pitfalls and an examiner-style mark scheme.

strands.strand: Speaking: stimulus-card discussion and individual research project
strands.gapNote: No app supports the A-level speaking exam (theme stimulus card debate or independent research project presentation).

suggestedApps.title: Stimulus Debate Arena
suggestedApps.concept: Projects an A-level theme stimulus card, generates probing examiner questions, and times an extended response while suggesting counter-arguments to push spontaneity.

strands.strand: Cultural, social and political themes of target-language societies
strands.gapNote: Literary analysis touches theme, but the four A-level societal/political themes (immigration, politics, cyber-society, occupation/resistance) are not addressed as content.

suggestedApps.title: Theme Dossier Builder
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive knowledge organiser for each A-level theme where pupils tag authentic articles and clips with arguments and statistics to build an essay-ready evidence bank.

summary.headline: Grammar, vocabulary and phonics are well covered (strongest at KS3-KS4), but interactive speaking and reading/translation comprehension are the biggest gaps right across the phases, with culture/intercultural understanding largely absent from KS2 to KS5.

subject: Drama
sourceNotes: Drama is not a standalone National Curriculum subject in England: it sits within the EYFS framework (Expressive Arts and Design; Communication and Language), within English at KS1-KS3 (spoken language: role-play, improvisation, performing scripts, reading aloud, drama and performance), and at KS4/KS5 is defined by exam-board GCSE and A-level specifications. Strands assembled from: EYFS statutory framework; English NC 2014 spoken-language and reading programmes of study (KS1-3); and the GCSE/A-level Drama and Theatre exam specifications (AQA 8261, Pearson Edexcel 1DR0 GCSE and 9DR0 A-level, Eduqas), verified via web search May 2026. App list from /tmp/subject_apps.json key "Drama".

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (within EYFS framework: Expressive Arts and Design; Communication and Language) - drama/role-play, not a named subject

strands.strand: Imaginative and role play
Expressing emotions and feelings through performance
Storytelling and acting out narratives
Movement, gesture and physical expression
strands.gapNote: Emotion masks touch facial expression but no app explores whole-body movement, mime or gesture for the youngest children.

suggestedApps.title: Move Like a...
suggestedApps.concept: Tap an animal or character card and the on-screen figure animates the movement, then children copy and the class votes which body shape matches best.

strands.strand: Singing, rhyme and musical performance
strands.gapNote: No app links music, song or rhythm to performance for EYFS.

suggestedApps.title: Action Song Stage
suggestedApps.concept: Project a nursery rhyme with tappable action icons; children perform the matching gesture as each line lights up, building a whole-class performance.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory within English (spoken language: role-play, improvising, participating in performances and discussions)

strands.strand: Role play and improvisation
Still image and tableau (freeze frames)
Storytelling and performing narratives
Voice, expression and speaking aloud
strands.gapNote: Puppet theatre lets children speak in role but no app explicitly develops vocal expression, volume, pace or clarity.

suggestedApps.title: Voice Dial
suggestedApps.concept: A dial sets loud/soft, fast/slow, happy/sad; the class reads the same line and listens back, matching the voice to the meaning.

strands.strand: Characterisation and emotions in role
strands.gapNote: Freeze frames and puppets imply character but no app focuses on building a character's feelings and motives at KS1 level.

suggestedApps.title: Character Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Drag a feeling, a want and a problem onto a character card, then the whole class acts the resulting mini-scene and discusses what changed.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory within English (spoken language: improvising, devising, scripting, performing plays and poems aloud)

strands.strand: Improvisation and devising
Scriptwriting and play texts
Performing scripts and poems aloud
strands.gapNote: Script Writer produces text but no app supports rehearsing and performing a script aloud with expression and cues.

suggestedApps.title: Read-Aloud Rehearsal Room
suggestedApps.concept: Highlights each character's lines in turn with pace and pause markers; the class performs a script extract while the tool keeps the cueing.

strands.strand: Characterisation, status and role
strands.gapNote: The spinner randomises characters but no app deliberately develops character, status or hot-seating.

suggestedApps.title: Hot Seat Generator
suggestedApps.concept: Pick a character; the screen serves probing questions for hot-seating while peers vote whether the answer stays in role.

strands.strand: Drama techniques and conventions (thought-tracking, narration, mime)
strands.gapNote: Improv scenarios are covered but the named conventions (thought-tracking, narration, mime, conscience alley) are not taught explicitly.

suggestedApps.title: Convention Cards
suggestedApps.concept: Flip a card to reveal a technique with a worked example animation, then the class applies it to a shared stimulus on screen.

strands.strand: Responding to and evaluating performance
strands.gapNote: No KS2 app helps pupils give structured feedback on a peer performance.

suggestedApps.title: Two Stars and a Wish
suggestedApps.concept: After watching a clip the class drags strengths and one improvement onto a shared board, building a simple evaluation vocabulary.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory within English (spoken language: improvising, rehearsing, performing scripted and devised drama; analysing performance)

strands.strand: Devising original drama from stimulus
Staging, blocking and use of space
Drama techniques and conventions
Performing from a script and text interpretation
strands.gapNote: Blocking covers movement but no KS3 app focuses on interpreting and rehearsing a set script with subtext.

suggestedApps.title: Subtext Decoder
suggestedApps.concept: A scripted line shows on screen; students drag possible subtexts and intentions, then see how delivery changes the meaning.

strands.strand: Vocal and physical performance skills
strands.gapNote: No KS3 app systematically builds voice (projection, tone, pace) and physicality (posture, gesture, gait).

suggestedApps.title: Voice and Body Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Sliders for pitch, pace, projection and posture transform an on-screen actor; students match settings to a character brief.

strands.strand: Evaluating and responding to live/recorded theatre
strands.gapNote: Mastery Studio is pitched at KS5; KS3 lacks an age-appropriate tool for analysing a watched performance.

suggestedApps.title: Watch and Critique
suggestedApps.concept: Pause-point prompts overlay a clip asking about acting, design and impact; responses collate into a class review.

strands.strand: Design and technical theatre (set, lighting, sound, costume)

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (GCSE Drama: AQA 8261, Pearson Edexcel 1DR0, Eduqas)

strands.strand: Devising drama (and devising log)
strands.gapNote: Devising toolkit is KS3; no GCSE-specific tool supports the assessed devising process and the written devising log.

suggestedApps.title: Devising Log Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Guides students through stimulus, intentions, rehearsal and evaluation entries against AO1/AO2/AO4, exporting a structured log draft.

strands.strand: Performance from text
Theatre practitioners and theory
Study of a set text from a performance perspective
strands.gapNote: Mastery Studio analyses scripts but is pitched at KS5/A-level; no GCSE-level set-text-as-performer tool with the section B exam focus.

suggestedApps.title: Set Text Director
suggestedApps.concept: For a key extract, students assign staging, character intention and design choices to each beat, then generate model exam answers in the page-to-stage style.

strands.strand: Design and technical theatre (set, lighting, sound, costume)
strands.gapNote: No app addresses the design/technical option (lighting, sound, set, costume) assessed across GCSE specs.

suggestedApps.title: Tech Theatre Designer
suggestedApps.concept: Drag lighting states, sound cues, set pieces and costume choices onto a scene and see/justify how each shapes mood and meaning.

strands.strand: Reviewing live theatre
strands.gapNote: Live Theatre Review Builder exists but is labelled KS5; a GCSE-pitched version with the GCSE mark scheme is missing.

suggestedApps.title: GCSE Live Review Coach
suggestedApps.concept: Scaffolds a review around acting, design and audience impact with GCSE band descriptors and exemplar phrases.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (A-level Drama and Theatre: Pearson Edexcel 9DR0, AQA, Eduqas)

strands.strand: Theatre practitioners (in depth and comparative)
Set text analysis as performer, director and designer
Live theatre evaluation and review
Devising original theatre influenced by a practitioner
strands.gapNote: Mastery Studio covers conventions and application but no A-level tool drives the assessed devising-in-the-style-of-a-practitioner process and working notebook.

suggestedApps.title: Devising in Style Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Pick a practitioner; the tool constrains rehearsal choices to that style and scaffolds the working-notebook reflective entries.

strands.strand: Scripted performance and interpretation (monologue/group)
strands.gapNote: Monologue Coach is GCSE-pitched; no A-level-level interpretation tool for extended scripted performance and concept development.

suggestedApps.title: Interpretation Concept Board
suggestedApps.concept: Build a directorial concept for a scene with annotated staging, design and intention layers, then test it against the text's themes.

strands.strand: Theatre history, genres and styles in context
strands.gapNote: No app maps theatrical movements (Greek, Naturalism, Epic, Absurd, Physical) and their conventions and context.

suggestedApps.title: Theatre Timeline Explorer
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive timeline where clicking a movement reveals its conventions, key plays and a worked staging example for comparison.

summary.headline: Drama is broadly covered EYFS to KS5 but the biggest gap is GCSE/A-level design and technical theatre plus theatre history and genres, with many strands only partially served because several core tools are pitched at the wrong key stage.

subject: Art and Design
sourceNotes: No local knowledge file for this subject. Built from model knowledge of the statutory England National Curriculum 2014 programmes of study for Art and Design (statutory KS1-KS3), the EYFS statutory framework Expressive Arts and Design area of learning, and typical GCSE (e.g. AQA/Edexcel/OCR Art, Craft and Design) and A-level Art and Design exam-board subject content. Web tools were not used; these frameworks are broad and stable. App library checked: 209 files in apps/, none addressing Art and Design (the only near-hits were geography-earthquake-waves.html and mfl-ks1-colours-languages.html, neither an art app).

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (EYFS framework, Expressive Arts and Design area of learning)

strands.strand: Exploring materials, media and techniques
strands.gapNote: No app lets young children explore colour mixing, textures, tools or simple making.

suggestedApps.title: Magic Mixing Pots
suggestedApps.concept: Tap two paint pots and watch them swirl into a new colour live, with a wheel that predicts the result before children try it on the easel.

suggestedApps.title: Texture Treasure Hunt
suggestedApps.concept: Drag rubbing tools over hidden surfaces (bark, brick, leaf) to reveal the texture pattern, naming rough/smooth/bumpy as you go.

strands.strand: Being imaginative and expressive through art and design
strands.gapNote: Nothing supports child-initiated creating, representing ideas or talking about own work.

suggestedApps.title: My Picture Story
suggestedApps.concept: Children build a scene from stampable shapes and brushes, then the teacher taps each part so the child narrates what it is, building art-talk vocabulary.

strands.strand: Colour, shape and pattern recognition
strands.gapNote: No early art app teaches naming colours, simple shapes and repeating patterns.

suggestedApps.title: Pattern Train
suggestedApps.concept: Drag coloured carriages to continue a repeating AB/ABB pattern; the train chuffs along correctly only when the pattern holds.

strands.strand: Responding to and talking about art
strands.gapNote: No app prompts children to share feelings and observations about images and artists.

suggestedApps.title: How Does It Make You Feel?
suggestedApps.concept: Project a famous painting and let the class tap emotion faces and colour swatches that match the mood, sparking whole-carpet discussion.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (National Curriculum 2014 programme of study)

strands.strand: Drawing, painting and developing techniques (line, shape, form, space)
strands.gapNote: No app teaches the visual elements or lets pupils practise line, shape and form.

suggestedApps.title: Line Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils drag to make wavy, zigzag, thick and thin lines, then match each line type to a feeling and use it to draw a creature live.

suggestedApps.title: Shape to Form
suggestedApps.concept: Slider turns a flat 2D shape into a shaded 3D form, showing how light and tone make a circle become a sphere on the whiteboard.

strands.strand: Colour theory and colour mixing
strands.gapNote: Primary/secondary colour mixing is not covered by any art app.

suggestedApps.title: Colour Mixer Easel
suggestedApps.concept: Drag primaries together to make secondaries on an interactive palette, with a colour wheel that fills in as the class discovers each new colour.

strands.strand: Using materials creatively (collage, printmaking, sculpture, textiles)
strands.gapNote: No app supports designing and making with a range of materials and techniques.

suggestedApps.title: Print Press
suggestedApps.concept: Carve a simple stamp, ink it and press repeats to build a pattern, demonstrating how printmaking makes identical marks.

strands.strand: Imagination, observation and recording ideas (sketchbooks)
strands.gapNote: Nothing models recording observations or using a sketchbook to develop ideas.

suggestedApps.title: Observation Station
suggestedApps.concept: A photo fades to faint outline so pupils trace what they truly see, building looking-and-drawing habits the class can compare.

strands.strand: Knowledge of artists, craftspeople and designers
strands.gapNote: No app introduces named artists or links pupils' work to artists' techniques.

suggestedApps.title: Artist Detective
suggestedApps.concept: Reveal an artist's painting piece by piece while the class guesses the subject, then a one-tap fact card explains the artist and technique.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (National Curriculum 2014 programme of study)

strands.strand: Mastering drawing techniques (line, tone, perspective, proportion)
strands.gapNote: No app builds the increasing mastery of drawing expected at KS2.

suggestedApps.title: Vanishing Point
suggestedApps.concept: Drag a horizon line and vanishing point to watch a street snap into one-point perspective, teaching depth interactively.

suggestedApps.title: Tone Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils shade a sphere in five tonal bands and the app scores how smoothly the gradient runs from highlight to shadow.

strands.strand: Colour, pattern, texture and the visual elements
strands.gapNote: Colour relationships and visual-element vocabulary are not addressed.

suggestedApps.title: Colour Harmony Wheel
suggestedApps.concept: Spin the wheel to find complementary, analogous and warm/cool sets, previewing each scheme on a sample artwork live.

strands.strand: Painting, printmaking, sculpture and textiles techniques
strands.gapNote: No app demonstrates the range of making techniques and materials.

suggestedApps.title: Clay Studio 3D
suggestedApps.concept: Pinch, coil and slab a virtual pot on the board, rotating it 360 degrees to show how 3D form is built up.

strands.strand: Developing ideas and reviewing work in sketchbooks
strands.gapNote: Nothing supports planning, refining and self-evaluating a piece of work.

suggestedApps.title: Idea Web Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Build a digital mood board from a theme word, then drag the strongest ideas into a development sequence the class critiques together.

strands.strand: Great artists, architects and designers in history
strands.gapNote: No app teaches art movements or the work of significant artists and designers.

suggestedApps.title: Movement Timeline
suggestedApps.concept: Scrub a timeline from Renaissance to Pop Art; each stop morphs a sample image into that style so pupils see the movements change.

strands.strand: Evaluating and analysing creative works (art critique)
strands.gapNote: Structured looking at and discussing artworks is not supported.

suggestedApps.title: Critique Lens
suggestedApps.concept: Annotate a projected artwork with composition lines, colour callouts and mood tags using a guided describe-analyse-interpret-judge frame.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (National Curriculum 2014 programme of study)

strands.strand: Drawing and recording from observation, experience and imagination
strands.gapNote: No app develops the observational drawing and recording expected at KS3.

suggestedApps.title: Sight-Size Drawing
suggestedApps.concept: Overlay a measuring grid and plumb lines on a still-life photo so pupils learn to check angles and proportion as they draw.

strands.strand: Mastery of media, materials and techniques (2D and 3D)
strands.gapNote: Increasing proficiency across a wide range of media is not addressed.

suggestedApps.title: Media Mixer
suggestedApps.concept: Switch a single drawing between charcoal, ink wash, lino print and collage to compare how each medium changes mark and mood.

strands.strand: Formal elements and composition (line, tone, colour, form, space)
strands.gapNote: Composition and the formal elements as a deliberate design tool are uncovered.

suggestedApps.title: Composition Grid
suggestedApps.concept: Drag focal points onto rule-of-thirds and golden-ratio overlays to see how placement changes the balance of a photo or painting.

strands.strand: Developing ideas through investigation: sketchbooks and the design process
strands.gapNote: No app models artist research, idea development and personal response.

suggestedApps.title: Project Journey
suggestedApps.concept: Walk a class through a real GCSE-style project flow from artist research to final piece, dragging evidence into each assessment objective.

strands.strand: History and contexts of art, craft and design (movements and cultures)
strands.gapNote: Art movements, periods and cultural contexts are not taught by any app.

suggestedApps.title: Style Decoder
suggestedApps.concept: Upload or pick an image and the app highlights the visual cues that place it in Cubism, Impressionism, Surrealism and more, with movement fact cards.

strands.strand: Critical analysis and evaluation of own and others' work
strands.gapNote: Structured critique and self-evaluation against criteria is not supported.

suggestedApps.title: Gallery Critique
suggestedApps.concept: Anonymous peer work is projected and the class tags strengths and next steps against the assessment objectives in a live gallery walk.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (GCSE Art and Design specifications, e.g. AQA, Edexcel, OCR)

strands.strand: Developing ideas through investigations and artist research (AO1)
strands.gapNote: No app supports the GCSE AO1 research-and-development requirement.

suggestedApps.title: AO1 Research Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Scaffold a contextual study page: drag artist images, annotate influences and link findings to the pupil's own intentions.

strands.strand: Refining work by exploring media, materials and techniques (AO2)
strands.gapNote: Media experimentation and refinement for AO2 is not addressed.

suggestedApps.title: Experiment Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Run side-by-side media trials on one motif and tag which experiments to take forward, modelling AO2 refinement decisions.

strands.strand: Recording ideas, observations and insights (AO3)
strands.gapNote: Observational recording and reflective annotation for AO3 is uncovered.

suggestedApps.title: Observation Logbook
suggestedApps.concept: Timed observational drawing challenges paired with prompted annotations that meet AO3 recording expectations.

strands.strand: Presenting a personal and meaningful response: the final piece (AO4)
strands.gapNote: Planning and realising a coherent final outcome for AO4 is not supported.

suggestedApps.title: Final Piece Planner
suggestedApps.concept: Assemble compositional studies into a mock-up of the final piece and check it connects back to AO1-AO3 evidence.

strands.strand: Formal elements, composition and visual language at depth
strands.gapNote: Advanced use of the formal elements is not taught by any app.

suggestedApps.title: Visual Language Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Manipulate colour, tone, scale and texture on a sample work and instantly see how meaning and mood shift.

strands.strand: Critical and contextual understanding (movements, cultures, careers)
strands.gapNote: No app builds the contextual knowledge underpinning GCSE Art and Design.

suggestedApps.title: Context Connector
suggestedApps.concept: Map an artwork to its movement, social context and a modern creative career, showing why context matters in coursework.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (A-level Art and Design specifications, e.g. AQA, Edexcel, OCR)

strands.strand: Independent project development and sustained enquiry
strands.gapNote: No app scaffolds the self-directed, sustained enquiry central to A-level.

suggestedApps.title: Enquiry Tracker
suggestedApps.concept: Plot a personal investigation as a living map of questions, experiments and decisions students update across the project.

strands.strand: Advanced media, materials and technical mastery
strands.gapNote: Sophisticated, controlled use of media and processes is not supported.

suggestedApps.title: Technique Masterclass
suggestedApps.concept: Step-through demos of advanced processes (glazing, etching, mixed media) with parameter sliders to see each variable's effect.

strands.strand: Critical, contextual and theoretical analysis
strands.gapNote: Deep critical analysis and the related study/personal study essay are uncovered.

suggestedApps.title: Personal Study Workbench
suggestedApps.concept: Build an evidenced visual essay, linking analysis, quotation and the student's practice with structure prompts for the related study.

strands.strand: Personal investigation: synthesising ideas into resolved outcomes
strands.gapNote: Synthesis of research into resolved personal outcomes is not modelled.

suggestedApps.title: Synthesis Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Cluster research strands and pull threads together into a planned resolved outcome, exposing the synthesis examiners reward.

strands.strand: Connections between practice, contexts and contemporary art
strands.gapNote: Situating personal work within contemporary and historical practice is uncovered.

suggestedApps.title: Contemporary Compass
suggestedApps.concept: Position a student's work on axes of medium, concept and era against living artists to find genuine, defensible connections.

summary.headline: There is zero app coverage of Art and Design across all phases EYFS to KS5; the most urgent gap is the absence of any tool for the core making and visual-elements strands (drawing, colour and media) that run through every key stage.

subject: Music
sourceNotes: Statutory programmes of study confirmed via gov.uk (National curriculum in England: music programmes of study, 2013/2014) and the DfE non-statutory Model Music Curriculum KS1-3 (March 2021). EYFS framed against the EYFS statutory framework (Expressive Arts and Design: Being Imaginative and Expressive; Communication and Language). KS4/KS5 framed against typical GCSE and A-level Music exam-board subject content (AQA, Edexcel, Eduqas/OCR). Coverage cross-referenced against the WeST app library, which currently contains zero Music apps. Strand structure drawn from the inter-related dimensions of music plus the performing/composing/listening pillars.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (EYFS framework: Expressive Arts and Design, plus Communication and Language)

strands.strand: Singing songs, rhymes and chants
strands.gapNote: No app supports learning, joining in with or performing nursery rhymes and simple songs.

suggestedApps.title: Sing-Along Story Time
suggestedApps.concept: Animated nursery-rhyme player with bouncing-ball lyric highlighting and a record-yourself-singing button the class can play back together.

suggestedApps.title: Pat-a-Cake Clap Map
suggestedApps.concept: Tap the screen in time with familiar rhymes; visual beat dots light up so children see steady pulse while they chant.

strands.strand: Exploring sounds and instruments (timbre)
strands.gapNote: Nothing lets children explore and match the sounds of percussion instruments and everyday objects.

suggestedApps.title: What Made That Sound?
suggestedApps.concept: Tap a hidden instrument, hear it, then drag the matching picture; reveals shaker, drum, bells, triangle with big projectable visuals.

suggestedApps.title: Loud and Quiet Garden
suggestedApps.concept: Touch animals and objects to make loud or soft sounds, sorting them into a loud box and a quiet box to introduce dynamics.

strands.strand: Keeping a steady beat and moving to music
strands.gapNote: No app builds pulse awareness through clapping, tapping and movement.

suggestedApps.title: March, Tiptoe, Stomp
suggestedApps.concept: Music changes tempo and the on-screen character marches, tiptoes or stomps; class copies the movement to feel fast and slow.

suggestedApps.title: Heartbeat Drum
suggestedApps.concept: A giant drum that pulses at a chosen tempo; children tap along and a smiley meter rewards staying in time.

strands.strand: Listening and responding to music
strands.gapNote: Nothing prompts children to listen attentively and respond to mood and contrast in short pieces.

suggestedApps.title: How Does It Feel?
suggestedApps.concept: Plays a short musical clip then asks children to tap a happy, sleepy, spooky or busy face, opening simple talk about musical mood.

strands.strand: Making and creating music (early composing)
strands.gapNote: No app lets children invent simple sound patterns or sequences.

suggestedApps.title: Sound Story Maker
suggestedApps.concept: Drag weather, animal and instrument sounds onto a timeline to build a sound story for a picture book the teacher reads aloud.

suggestedApps.title: Colour Music Pad
suggestedApps.concept: A big grid of coloured pads, each a different note or sound; children improvise patterns and the teacher records favourites.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (National Curriculum 2014 programme of study for music)

strands.strand: Singing and using the voice expressively
strands.gapNote: No app supports tunefully singing songs, chants and rhymes or vocal expression.

suggestedApps.title: Pitch Caterpillar
suggestedApps.concept: Children sing into the mic and a caterpillar climbs higher for higher notes, giving instant visual feedback on matching pitch.

suggestedApps.title: Call and Response Karaoke
suggestedApps.concept: Teacher-led echo singing where the app sings a phrase and a meter shows how closely the class echoed it back in tune.

strands.strand: Playing tuned and untuned instruments
strands.gapNote: Nothing lets pupils play simple rhythmic and melodic parts on classroom instruments.

suggestedApps.title: Glockenspiel Hero
suggestedApps.concept: On-screen glockenspiel with colour-coded bars; scrolling colour cues let the whole class play a simple melody together.

suggestedApps.title: Percussion Pattern Band
suggestedApps.concept: Assign claves, tambourine and drum to groups; the app cues each group in turn to layer up a class rhythm performance.

strands.strand: Exploring the dimensions of music (pitch, duration, dynamics, tempo, timbre)
strands.gapNote: No app teaches high/low, long/short, loud/quiet, fast/slow or instrument sounds interactively.

suggestedApps.title: High Low Slider
suggestedApps.concept: Drag a balloon up and down and the pitch rises and falls, so children hear and see the link between visual height and pitch.

suggestedApps.title: Loud-Soft-Fast-Slow Mixer
suggestedApps.concept: Four big dials the class turns to change dynamics and tempo of a backing track in real time to feel each dimension.

strands.strand: Listening with concentration to a range of music
strands.gapNote: Nothing structures attentive listening to live and recorded music across styles.

suggestedApps.title: Listening Detectives
suggestedApps.concept: Plays a clip and pupils tap when they spot an instrument or a change in speed; a scoreboard celebrates careful listeners.

suggestedApps.title: Spot the Family
suggestedApps.concept: Children hear an instrument and drag it into strings, brass, woodwind or percussion to begin classifying timbre.

strands.strand: Experimenting, creating and combining sounds (composing)
strands.gapNote: No app supports inventing and combining sounds to create simple compositions.

suggestedApps.title: Sound Soup
suggestedApps.concept: Drag sounds into a pot and stir to combine them, then play back the mix to explore texture and layering.

suggestedApps.title: Storm Composer
suggestedApps.concept: Class builds a weather soundscape by layering rain, wind and thunder loops, controlling when each enters and fades.

strands.strand: Introducing simple notation and symbols
strands.gapNote: Nothing introduces graphic scores or simple symbols to represent sounds.

suggestedApps.title: Draw Your Music
suggestedApps.concept: Children draw lines and shapes that the app turns into sound, building the idea that symbols can represent music.

suggestedApps.title: Long and Short Notes
suggestedApps.concept: Drag big and small note blocks onto a beat grid and hear long versus short sounds to introduce duration notation.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (National Curriculum 2014 programme of study for music)

strands.strand: Singing and performing in solo and ensemble contexts
strands.gapNote: No app supports singing in unison and parts or performing with confidence and control.

suggestedApps.title: Round Robin Rounds
suggestedApps.concept: Teaches singing rounds: the app holds each group's part with a moving cursor so two and three-part rounds stay together.

suggestedApps.title: Ensemble Conductor
suggestedApps.concept: Projected score where the teacher taps to bring in voices or instruments, training pupils to perform as an ensemble.

strands.strand: Playing instruments with increasing accuracy and control
strands.gapNote: Nothing supports developing technique on tuned/untuned instruments or whole-class instrumental teaching.

suggestedApps.title: Recorder Trainer
suggestedApps.concept: Fingering charts plus a tuner that listens and confirms each note, scaffolding whole-class recorder or wind teaching.

suggestedApps.title: Riff Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Loop-based instrument practice where pupils lock in a riff at slow tempo then speed it up, with accuracy feedback.

strands.strand: Composing and improvising music
strands.gapNote: No app lets pupils improvise and compose for a range of purposes using the dimensions of music.

suggestedApps.title: Pentatonic Improviser
suggestedApps.concept: A five-note pentatonic pad over a backing groove so any combination sounds good, freeing pupils to improvise confidently.

suggestedApps.title: Melody Sketchpad
suggestedApps.concept: Drag notes onto a stave-lite grid to compose an eight-bar melody, hear it instantly and tweak rhythm and pitch.

strands.strand: Listening, appraising and developing understanding (dimensions of music)
strands.gapNote: Nothing develops analytical listening using texture, structure, tempo, dynamics and timbre vocabulary.

suggestedApps.title: Texture Layers Explorer
suggestedApps.concept: Strip out and add back instrumental layers of a real track so the class hears and labels thin versus thick texture.

suggestedApps.title: Form Mapper
suggestedApps.concept: As a song plays, the timeline auto-labels verse, chorus and bridge so pupils visualise musical structure and form.

strands.strand: Reading and using staff and other musical notations
strands.gapNote: No app teaches reading staff notation, note names, rhythms and musical symbols.

suggestedApps.title: Stave Climber
suggestedApps.concept: Notes appear on the treble stave and pupils name them or tap the matching keyboard key, levelling up through note ranges.

suggestedApps.title: Rhythm Read-Off
suggestedApps.concept: Scrolling rhythm notation (crotchets, quavers, rests) that the class claps in time, with a hit-detector marking accuracy.

strands.strand: History of music and great composers and musicians
strands.gapNote: Nothing introduces the history of music across periods, traditions and great composers.

suggestedApps.title: Music Through Time
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive timeline from Baroque to today; tap an era to hear a defining clip and meet a key composer or musician.

suggestedApps.title: Whose Sound Is This?
suggestedApps.concept: Listening quiz that places famous works in their period and tradition, building chronological and stylistic knowledge.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (National Curriculum 2014 programme of study for music)

strands.strand: Performing: singing and playing with technical control and expression
strands.gapNote: No app supports playing and performing fluently on instruments and voice with control and expression.

suggestedApps.title: Keyboard Skills Studio
suggestedApps.concept: On-screen keyboard with chord-shape overlays and scrolling notation so pupils build performance pieces hands-together.

suggestedApps.title: Backing Track Performer
suggestedApps.concept: Play or sing over genre backing tracks with a click and a tuner, recording takes to self-assess accuracy and expression.

strands.strand: Composing and improvising across styles and genres
strands.gapNote: Nothing supports composing using staves and technologies, drawing on a range of musical structures and styles.

suggestedApps.title: DAW Lite Composer
suggestedApps.concept: A simplified multitrack sequencer to layer drums, bass, chords and melody, teaching arranging and the elements of composition.

suggestedApps.title: Chord Progression Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Drag chords into a grid, hear the progression instantly and audition cadences to learn harmony by ear and by theory.

strands.strand: Listening, analysing and appraising music with understanding
strands.gapNote: No app develops critical listening and analysis using the elements of music and technical vocabulary.

suggestedApps.title: Active Listening Annotator
suggestedApps.concept: A waveform with tap-to-tag markers for tempo, dynamic and instrumentation changes, building analytical listening skills.

suggestedApps.title: Genre Decoder
suggestedApps.concept: Compare clips side by side and identify the features that define blues, reggae, EDM and more for stylistic analysis.

strands.strand: Music theory, notation and the elements of music
strands.gapNote: Nothing teaches staff notation, keys, scales, intervals, chords and rhythm theory at KS3 depth.

suggestedApps.title: Theory Trainer
suggestedApps.concept: Adaptive drills on note reading, key signatures, scales and intervals with a built-in keyboard for instant aural checking.

suggestedApps.title: Scale and Chord Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Construct major, minor and modal scales and triads on a virtual keyboard, hearing and seeing how they are formed.

strands.strand: Music technology, recording and production
strands.gapNote: No app introduces sequencing, sampling, recording and production used in modern music-making.

suggestedApps.title: Beat Maker Sequencer
suggestedApps.concept: A step sequencer for programming drum patterns and basslines, teaching how electronic and pop grooves are built.

suggestedApps.title: Sample and Loop Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Chop, pitch and arrange samples into a track, demonstrating sampling and looping as compositional tools.

strands.strand: Music history, traditions and world musics
strands.gapNote: Nothing covers the development of music across periods, cultures and global traditions.

suggestedApps.title: World Music Atlas
suggestedApps.concept: A clickable world map that plays gamelan, samba, raga and West African drumming with the features that define each tradition.

suggestedApps.title: Eras and Innovations
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive history of Western and popular music linking each era's sound to its instruments, technology and society.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (GCSE Music: AQA, Edexcel, Eduqas, OCR); music is not statutory at KS4 but the NC entitlement to study an arts subject applies

strands.strand: Performing (solo and ensemble) for assessment
strands.gapNote: No app supports the GCSE performance component, technique, accuracy and expression under recording conditions.

suggestedApps.title: Performance Logbook
suggestedApps.concept: Record performances against a click, self-mark on accuracy, fluency and expression using the GCSE descriptors, and track progress.

suggestedApps.title: Ensemble Sync Trainer
suggestedApps.concept: Practise a part against the missing ensemble lines with tempo control to build accurate, balanced ensemble playing.

strands.strand: Composing to a brief and free composition
strands.gapNote: Nothing scaffolds the GCSE composition coursework: briefs, development, structure and notated/produced submissions.

suggestedApps.title: Composition Brief Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Generates GCSE-style briefs then guides through melody, harmony, structure and development with audio playback at each step.

suggestedApps.title: Harmony and Cadence Coach
suggestedApps.concept: Audition chord choices and cadences against a melody to teach functional harmony for composition coursework.

strands.strand: Listening and appraising set works and unfamiliar music
strands.gapNote: No app trains the listening exam: analysing set works and unfamiliar extracts across areas of study.

suggestedApps.title: Set Work Dissector
suggestedApps.concept: Synced score-and-audio playback that highlights key features of GCSE set works for analysis and revision.

suggestedApps.title: Unfamiliar Extract Trainer
suggestedApps.concept: Exam-style dictation and analysis questions on unheard clips, marking responses on tonality, metre and instrumentation.

strands.strand: Areas of study and musical contexts (genres, styles, traditions)
strands.gapNote: Nothing covers the GCSE areas of study spanning Western classical, popular, fusion and world music contexts.

suggestedApps.title: Areas of Study Explorer
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive breakdown of each GCSE area of study with defining features, key works and listening examples.

suggestedApps.title: Style Feature Flashcards
suggestedApps.concept: Adaptive aural flashcards: hear a clip, identify the area of study and justify with two musical features.

strands.strand: Music theory, harmony and notation for GCSE
strands.gapNote: No app consolidates the theory, harmony, rhythm and notation underpinning all three GCSE components.

suggestedApps.title: GCSE Theory Bootcamp
suggestedApps.concept: Targeted drills on keys, scales, chords, intervals and rhythmic dictation with instant aural confirmation.

suggestedApps.title: Score Reading Trainer
suggestedApps.concept: Follow full scores in real time and answer pinpoint questions on what is happening in each part at a given bar.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (A-level Music and Music Technology: AQA, Edexcel, Eduqas, OCR); post-16, not part of the statutory National Curriculum

strands.strand: Advanced performing (recital and ensemble)
strands.gapNote: No app supports A-level recital-standard performance preparation, interpretation and recording.

suggestedApps.title: Recital Prep Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Programme planning, take recording against a click and self-assessment on interpretation, stylistic awareness and technical command.

strands.strand: Advanced composition and arranging
strands.gapNote: Nothing scaffolds free and brief-based A-level composition, including stylistic pastiche and orchestration.

suggestedApps.title: Pastiche Composer
suggestedApps.concept: Compose in Bach chorale or other set styles with rule-checking that flags parallel fifths and weak voice-leading in real time.

suggestedApps.title: Orchestration Workshop
suggestedApps.concept: Reassign a melody across instrument families and hear the result, teaching ranges, transposition and orchestral colour.

strands.strand: Listening, analysis and appraising set works
strands.gapNote: No app supports in-depth A-level analysis of prescribed works and dictation under exam conditions.

suggestedApps.title: Analytical Score Annotator
suggestedApps.concept: Synced score and audio with harmonic, structural and motivic annotation tools for deep set-work study.

suggestedApps.title: Aural Dictation Pro
suggestedApps.concept: Multi-stage melodic and rhythmic dictation and harmonic identification at A-level difficulty, with mark-scheme feedback.

strands.strand: Music history, style and contextual study
strands.gapNote: Nothing covers the historical, stylistic and cultural context required for A-level areas of study.

suggestedApps.title: Style Period Deep Dive
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive study of each prescribed area's history, conventions and influential figures with comparative listening.

strands.strand: Advanced harmony, counterpoint and theory
strands.gapNote: No app develops the advanced harmony, counterpoint and analytical theory expected at A-level.

suggestedApps.title: Harmony and Counterpoint Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Realise figured bass and write species counterpoint with live audio and automated checking of voice-leading rules.

strands.strand: Music technology: recording, sequencing and production (Music Tech route)
strands.gapNote: Nothing covers the A-level Music Technology skills of recording, sequencing, mixing and production techniques.

suggestedApps.title: Mixdown Mastermind
