suggestedApps.concept: Interactive mixing desk where students balance, EQ and apply effects to stems, learning production decisions by ear.

suggestedApps.title: Recording Signal Path
suggestedApps.concept: Drag-and-build a studio signal chain (mic to interface to DAW) to learn capture, routing and recording theory.

strands.strand: Aural skills and dictation

summary.headline: Music has zero app coverage across every key stage from EYFS to KS5, so the entire subject is a gap; the most urgent priority is the statutory KS1-KS3 pillars of performing, composing, listening/appraising and notation, where there is currently nothing to project or manipulate in class.

subject: Computing
sourceNotes: No local curriculum summary or web tools used; mapped from model knowledge of the statutory England National Curriculum for Computing (2014 programmes of study, KS1-KS4), the EYFS statutory framework (Understanding the World / technology-related early learning), and typical OCR/AQA GCSE Computer Science and A-level Computer Science subject content. App library checked directly at /Users/nathanaelparnell/CCP2/curriculum-expert-web/apps/ (209 files): zero apps address the Computing curriculum, so every strand is coverage="none".

keyStages.statutoryNote: non-statutory (technology in everyday life sits within Understanding the World; explicit technology ELG removed from 2021 framework, but digital/technology learning remains good practice)

strands.strand: Technology in the everyday world
strands.gapNote: No app helps young children recognise and talk about everyday technology (tills, cameras, traffic lights, phones).

suggestedApps.title: Spot the Technology
suggestedApps.concept: Tap-to-reveal scene of a home and high street; children find devices that use technology and hear what each one does.

strands.strand: Early control and cause-and-effect (programmable toys)
strands.gapNote: Nothing models giving simple instructions to a Bee-Bot style toy or button-press cause and effect.

suggestedApps.title: Bee-Bot Floor Path
suggestedApps.concept: Drag forward/turn arrows onto a giant floor mat, press Go and watch the bee buzz the route; class predicts where it lands.

strands.strand: Following and giving simple instructions (early algorithms)
strands.gapNote: No play-based sequencing activity (first/next/then) to build precise instruction-giving.

suggestedApps.title: Make a Jam Sandwich Robot
suggestedApps.concept: Children drag step cards in order; a literal robot follows them exactly, getting it hilariously wrong if a step is missing.

strands.strand: Staying safe with devices (early online safety)
strands.gapNote: No age-appropriate prompt on safe screen use and telling a trusted adult.

suggestedApps.title: Ask a Grown-Up
suggestedApps.concept: Friendly characters meet pop-ups and unknown buttons; class votes whether to tap or fetch a trusted adult.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Algorithms: understanding precise step-by-step instructions
strands.gapNote: No app teaching that algorithms are unambiguous ordered instructions, and predicting their behaviour.

suggestedApps.title: Algorithm Detective
suggestedApps.concept: Class debugs a wonky set of get-dressed instructions, reordering steps until the on-screen character is correctly dressed.

strands.strand: Creating and debugging simple programs
strands.gapNote: No block-coding sandbox for writing and fixing simple programs at KS1 level.

suggestedApps.title: ScratchJr Stage
suggestedApps.concept: Drag picture-blocks to make a sprite move and speak; deliberate bugs are seeded so the class hunts and fixes them together.

strands.strand: Logical reasoning and prediction (sequence and outcome)
strands.gapNote: Nothing asks pupils to predict the behaviour of a given simple program before running it.

suggestedApps.title: Predict the Path
suggestedApps.concept: Show a short command sequence for a robot on a grid; pupils vote on the end square, then reveal the run.

strands.strand: Using technology purposefully (create, store, retrieve digital content)
strands.gapNote: No app modelling saving, opening and organising digital work (files/folders) at infant level.

suggestedApps.title: My Digital Drawer
suggestedApps.concept: Children create a picture, name and save it, then find it again later; teaches the save-name-retrieve loop visually.

strands.strand: Using technology safely and respectfully (online safety KS1)
strands.gapNote: No KS1 e-safety tool on private information and where to go for help.

suggestedApps.title: Keep It Private
suggestedApps.concept: Sorting game: drag facts into Share or Keep Secret buckets, with instant gentle feedback and a who-to-tell signpost.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Programming: sequence, selection, repetition and variables
strands.gapNote: No app teaching loops, if-statements and variables in a Scratch-style environment for juniors.

suggestedApps.title: Loop Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Live Scratch-style canvas where dragging a repeat block visibly tightens a sprite's spiral; class tunes the count and condition.

suggestedApps.title: Variable Scoreboard
suggestedApps.concept: Build a simple game where a score variable changes on collision; pupils watch the number update in real time.

strands.strand: Designing, writing and debugging programs to solve problems
strands.gapNote: Nothing supports decomposing a goal into a program and systematically debugging it.

suggestedApps.title: Decompose-It
suggestedApps.concept: Break a maze-solving challenge into sub-goals on sticky notes, then assemble them into working code with a step-through debugger.

strands.strand: Computational thinking: decomposition, abstraction and logical reasoning
strands.gapNote: No app explicitly drilling decomposition, pattern-spotting and abstraction as thinking skills.

suggestedApps.title: Pattern Spotter
suggestedApps.concept: Class finds the repeating rule in a sequence of tile patterns and generalises it into a single rule (abstraction made visible).

strands.strand: Computer networks, the internet and the World Wide Web
strands.gapNote: No app explaining networks, servers, routers and how a web page is requested and delivered.

suggestedApps.title: Journey of a Web Request
suggestedApps.concept: Animated packet travels from a pupil's device through router, ISP and server and back; class clicks each hop to learn its job.

strands.strand: Data and information: collecting, analysing, evaluating and presenting
strands.gapNote: No app on using spreadsheets/databases to collect, sort and present data and assess plausibility.

suggestedApps.title: Data Detectives
suggestedApps.concept: Class collects live class data into a mini-spreadsheet, sorts and charts it, then spots an implausible (faked) value.

strands.strand: Online safety, digital literacy and identifying unacceptable behaviour
strands.gapNote: No KS2 app on respectful use, recognising risk, and reporting content/contact concerns.

suggestedApps.title: Spot the Red Flag
suggestedApps.concept: Realistic chat/message scenarios; class flags risky messages and chooses report/block/tell, with reasoning feedback.

strands.strand: Selecting and combining software for creative digital projects
strands.gapNote: Nothing demonstrates combining tools (audio, image, text) to meet a brief on a range of devices.

suggestedApps.title: Multimedia Maker
suggestedApps.concept: Drag-and-drop project canvas where pupils combine image, sound and text layers to build a campaign poster against a brief.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Computational thinking and algorithm design
strands.gapNote: No app on designing, comparing and tracing algorithms (incl. searching and sorting) with abstraction.

suggestedApps.title: Sort Race
suggestedApps.concept: Animate bubble vs insertion sort side by side on the same data; class counts comparisons and predicts the winner.

strands.strand: Programming in a text-based language (e.g. Python)
strands.gapNote: No interactive Python sandbox covering sequence, selection, iteration, functions and data structures.

suggestedApps.title: Python Trace Theatre
suggestedApps.concept: Step a short Python program one line at a time while variable boxes update live; class predicts each next line.

suggestedApps.title: Function Factory
suggestedApps.concept: Build reusable functions with parameters/return values and watch the call stack visualised as nested boxes.

strands.strand: Data representation: binary, hexadecimal and storing media
strands.gapNote: No app on binary/denary/hex conversion and how text, images and sound are stored as bits.

suggestedApps.title: Binary Bulb Board
suggestedApps.concept: Eight light bulbs as place values; toggle bits and the denary/hex totals update live, with a binary-image pixel mode.

strands.strand: Computer systems: hardware, software and the CPU (fetch-decode-execute)
strands.gapNote: No app modelling Von Neumann architecture, memory and the fetch-decode-execute cycle.

suggestedApps.title: Inside the CPU
suggestedApps.concept: Animated CPU where class clicks through fetch, decode and execute, watching the program counter and registers change.

strands.strand: Networks, the internet and protocols
strands.gapNote: No app on network topologies, packet switching, IP/DNS and how the web works at KS3 depth.

suggestedApps.title: Packet Switch Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: Split a message into packets and route them across a drawn network; nodes can fail so class sees re-routing in action.

strands.strand: Boolean logic and logic circuits
strands.gapNote: No app for AND/OR/NOT gates, building circuits and reading truth tables.

suggestedApps.title: Logic Gate Workbench
suggestedApps.concept: Wire AND/OR/NOT gates on a board; flip inputs and the auto-generated truth table and output bulb update instantly.

strands.strand: Digital creativity and developing solutions to real problems
strands.gapNote: Nothing supports designing/evaluating a multi-tool digital artefact against user needs.

suggestedApps.title: Design-Build-Evaluate
suggestedApps.concept: A guided studio: pupils plan a digital product to a brief, prototype it, then run a structured user-test and iterate.

strands.strand: Online safety, digital resilience and responsible technology use
strands.gapNote: No KS3 app on privacy, digital footprint, misinformation and respectful/legal online conduct.

suggestedApps.title: Footprint Tracker
suggestedApps.concept: Simulated profile where each post/like leaves a visible trail; class sees how much can be inferred and tightens settings.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (NC requires Computing for all pupils KS4); GCSE Computer Science content is exam-board defined (OCR/AQA/Eduqas)

strands.strand: Systems architecture and memory (CPU, registers, primary/secondary storage)
strands.gapNote: No app on CPU components, registers, the FDE cycle, RAM/ROM/virtual memory and storage types.

suggestedApps.title: FDE Cycle Stepper
suggestedApps.concept: Step the fetch-decode-execute cycle with live register, MAR/MDR and program counter values; class predicts each register's next value.

strands.strand: Data representation (binary, hex, characters, images, sound, compression)
strands.gapNote: No app on number-base conversion, character sets, sampling, resolution and compression methods.

suggestedApps.title: Bits Behind the Image
suggestedApps.concept: Drag resolution and colour-depth sliders on a photo; file size recalculates live so class sees the storage trade-off.

strands.strand: Networks, topologies, protocols and the internet
strands.gapNote: No app on LAN/WAN, the TCP/IP and OSI layers, protocols, DNS and packet switching at GCSE depth.

suggestedApps.title: Protocol Stack Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Drag a message down the TCP/IP layers, watching headers added at each layer, then strip them off at the receiver.

strands.strand: Network and system security (threats and countermeasures)
strands.gapNote: No app on malware, phishing, social engineering, encryption and prevention measures.

suggestedApps.title: Caesar and Beyond
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive cipher wheel for Caesar/substitution encryption; class encrypts, then cracks intercepted messages by frequency analysis.

strands.strand: Algorithms (searching, sorting, computational thinking, trace tables)
strands.gapNote: No app to visualise linear/binary search and bubble/merge/insertion sort and complete trace tables.

suggestedApps.title: Search and Sort Visualiser
suggestedApps.concept: Run binary vs linear search on the same array, with comparison counts and a live-filling trace table.

strands.strand: Programming fundamentals and writing robust code
strands.gapNote: No app on sequence/selection/iteration, data types, arrays, subprograms, file handling and validation.

suggestedApps.title: Pseudocode to Python
suggestedApps.concept: Side-by-side panes translate exam-board pseudocode into runnable Python; class fixes broken validation routines live.

strands.strand: Boolean logic, data structures and databases (incl. SQL)
strands.gapNote: No app on logic gates/truth tables, arrays/records and simple SQL queries on a database.

suggestedApps.title: SQL Live Query
suggestedApps.concept: Type SELECT/WHERE queries against a sample school database and watch the result table filter instantly.

strands.strand: Impacts of digital technology (ethical, legal, environmental, privacy)
strands.gapNote: No app on legislation (Data Protection, Computer Misuse, Copyright), ethics and environmental impact.

suggestedApps.title: Tech Ethics Courtroom
suggestedApps.concept: Class judges real-style scenarios against the relevant Act, voting on legality and seeing the law that applies.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (A-level Computer Science, OCR/AQA); not statutory and optional at this phase

strands.strand: Programming paradigms and advanced techniques (OOP, recursion, functional)
strands.gapNote: No app demonstrating object-oriented, recursive and functional programming techniques interactively.

suggestedApps.title: Recursion Unrolled
suggestedApps.concept: Visualise the call stack of a recursive function (e.g. factorial, Fibonacci) growing and unwinding frame by frame.

strands.strand: Data structures and algorithms (trees, graphs, stacks, queues, Big-O)
strands.gapNote: No app on abstract data types and algorithm complexity (Dijkstra, A*, traversals, sorting efficiency).

suggestedApps.title: Graph Pathfinder
suggestedApps.concept: Draw a weighted graph and watch Dijkstra/A* explore nodes step by step, with the priority queue shown alongside.

strands.strand: Computer architecture, assembly and low-level operation
strands.gapNote: No app on assembly instructions, addressing modes, pipelining and the detailed FDE cycle.

suggestedApps.title: Little Man Computer
suggestedApps.concept: Write and run assembly in an LMC-style model; class watches the accumulator, mailboxes and program counter change per instruction.

strands.strand: Theory of computation (logic, finite state machines, Turing machines, regex)
strands.gapNote: No app on FSMs, regular expressions, BNF, computability and the limits of computation.

suggestedApps.title: FSM Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Drag states and transitions to build a finite state machine, then feed it strings to see accept/reject live.

strands.strand: Databases, big data and SQL (normalisation, transactions)
strands.gapNote: No app on relational design, normalisation (1NF-3NF), ACID transactions and advanced SQL.

suggestedApps.title: Normalise This
suggestedApps.concept: Drag attributes between tables to take a flat dataset from 1NF to 3NF, with redundancy highlighted as it disappears.

strands.strand: Networking, the internet and security (encryption, protocols, web tech)
strands.gapNote: No app on TCP/IP layers in depth, client-server, encryption (symmetric/asymmetric) and web technologies.

suggestedApps.title: Public Key Exchange
suggestedApps.concept: Animate asymmetric encryption: class encrypts with a public key and watches only the private key decrypt, with tampering demos.

strands.strand: Consequences and ethics of computing (legal, moral, cultural)
strands.gapNote: No app on the ethical/legal/cultural impacts of computing, AI bias and emerging technologies.

suggestedApps.title: Algorithm on Trial
suggestedApps.concept: Class interrogates a biased hiring/decision algorithm, adjusts its data and rules, and debates accountability.

strands.strand: Systematic software development (analysis, design, testing, maintenance)
strands.gapNote: No app on the development lifecycle, methodologies, testing strategies and project documentation.

suggestedApps.title: Lifecycle Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: Steer a project through analysis-design-build-test-maintain; injected requirement changes show why iteration matters.

summary.headline: Computing is entirely uncovered across all phases (EYFS to KS5): zero of 39 strands have any app, with the most urgent gaps being core programming (Scratch then Python) and data representation/binary, which underpin everything from KS1 to GCSE.

subject: Design and Technology
sourceNotes: No local curriculum summary file exists for this subject. Web tools were not used in this pass; strands were assembled from model knowledge of the statutory England National Curriculum programmes of study for Design and Technology (2014, KS1-KS3, including the discrete Cooking and Nutrition strand), the EYFS statutory framework (Expressive Arts and Design; Physical Development), and exam-board subject content for GCSE D&T, GCSE Food Preparation and Nutrition, and A-level Design and Technology. App coverage verified by listing /apps (209 files); no Design and Technology apps exist (the only loose name matches are science apps: science-circuit-builder.html, science-ks5-organic-mechanisms.html, science-ks2-food-chains.html).

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (within EYFS framework: Expressive Arts and Design, and Physical Development, not a discrete D&T subject)

strands.strand: Exploring and using materials, tools and techniques (Creating with Materials)
strands.gapNote: No app lets young children explore joining, shaping and constructing with everyday materials and simple tools.

suggestedApps.title: Junk Model Maker
suggestedApps.concept: Drag-and-drop a giant on-screen box of recycled materials (boxes, tubes, lids) and choose how to join them (tape, glue, slot) to build a model the class designs together.

suggestedApps.title: Tool Tap
suggestedApps.concept: Tap a tool (safe scissors, glue spreader, hole punch) and watch an animation of the correct, safe way to hold and use it, then match the tool to the job.

strands.strand: Constructing and building structures (blocks, kits, joining)
strands.gapNote: No app supports building and testing simple structures or talking about why a tower falls.

suggestedApps.title: Build It Up
suggestedApps.concept: Stack and arrange on-screen blocks to make a tower or bridge, then press a wobble button to test if it stays up and discuss why wide bases are stronger.

strands.strand: Designing and making for a purpose (planning, talking about what to make)
strands.gapNote: No app prompts children to say what they will make, who it is for and what it needs to do.

suggestedApps.title: What Shall We Make?
suggestedApps.concept: A picture-led planner where the class picks a purpose (a den for teddy, a boat that floats) and chooses materials, then compares the plan with the finished photo.

strands.strand: Food, cooking and healthy eating in the early years
strands.gapNote: No app introduces simple food preparation, hygiene and where food comes from for early years.

suggestedApps.title: Wash, Chop, Mix
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive kitchen where children tap the steps in order (wash hands, wash fruit, mix) to make a fruit salad, with hygiene reminders that pop up.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Design (purposeful, functional, appealing products for self and others; communicating ideas)
strands.gapNote: No app guides pupils to generate ideas and communicate a design for a chosen user and purpose.

suggestedApps.title: Design for a User
suggestedApps.concept: Class chooses a user (a baby, a postman) and a need, then drags features onto a product template and labels the design, talking through why each choice suits the user.

suggestedApps.title: Talk and Draw a Design
suggestedApps.concept: A shared sketch canvas with speech-bubble prompts that scaffold templates, mock-ups and ICT to communicate a design idea.

strands.strand: Make (selecting tools and materials; cutting, shaping, joining and finishing)
strands.gapNote: No app models choosing tools and the cut/shape/join/finish sequence at KS1.

suggestedApps.title: Choose Your Tools
suggestedApps.concept: Given a making job, pupils drag the right tools and materials from a tray, then order the make into cut, shape, join, finish steps and watch it assemble.

strands.strand: Evaluate (exploring existing products and judging their own against design criteria)
strands.gapNote: No app supports product evaluation against simple agreed criteria.

suggestedApps.title: Good Design Detective
suggestedApps.concept: Project a real product (a lunchbox, a coat) and let the class tick or cross it against criteria like easy to open, strong, fun, then judge their own make the same way.

strands.strand: Technical knowledge: simple structures (making structures stronger, stiffer, more stable)
strands.gapNote: No app demonstrates strengthening and stabilising simple structures.

suggestedApps.title: Strong, Stiff, Stable
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils fold, layer or brace an on-screen paper structure and a load-tester shows how each change lets it hold more weight.

strands.strand: Technical knowledge: simple mechanisms (levers, sliders, wheels and axles)
strands.gapNote: No app lets pupils manipulate basic mechanisms to make things move.

suggestedApps.title: Make It Move
suggestedApps.concept: Drag a slider or lever onto a moving picture (a peeping animal, a waving hand) and see the mechanism animate, exploring pivots and how far things move.

strands.strand: Cooking and nutrition (where food comes from; basic food preparation and hygiene)
strands.gapNote: No app teaches food origins and simple, safe preparation at KS1.

suggestedApps.title: Farm to Plate
suggestedApps.concept: Trace ingredients back to plant or animal sources with a tap-to-reveal map, then assemble a simple no-cook snack following hygiene steps.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Design (research, design criteria, communicating via sketches, cross-sections, exploded diagrams and CAD)
strands.gapNote: No app supports researching users and communicating designs through annotated sketches, diagrams or CAD at KS2.

suggestedApps.title: Design Brief Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Class researches a real user need, writes a design brief and specification, then drafts annotated sketches and a simple exploded diagram on a shared canvas.

suggestedApps.title: First Steps in CAD
suggestedApps.concept: A simplified 2D/3D modelling sandbox where pupils place and resize shapes to produce a printable net or plan, seeing how CAD speeds up redesign.

strands.strand: Make (precise tool use; range of materials and components; accurate measuring, marking, cutting)
strands.gapNote: No app models accurate measuring, marking out and component selection for KS2 making.

suggestedApps.title: Mark It Out
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils measure, mark and cut a virtual material to a tolerance, with a ruler and try-square overlay that flags inaccurate cuts before they commit.

strands.strand: Evaluate (investigating key events and individuals in D&T; evaluating against criteria and improving)
strands.gapNote: No app covers product investigation, design history or iterative improvement at KS2.

suggestedApps.title: Iterate and Improve
suggestedApps.concept: Test a prototype against the spec, log what failed, then adjust the design and re-test, building a visible improvement timeline.

suggestedApps.title: Designers Who Changed Things
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive gallery where pupils explore key designers and inventions and judge their impact against need and purpose.

strands.strand: Technical knowledge: structures (frame structures, reinforcing, triangulation)
strands.gapNote: No app demonstrates stronger frame structures and triangulation at KS2.

suggestedApps.title: Bridge Load Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Build a frame bridge, add triangulation and bracing, then drive increasing loads across it and watch where it bends or breaks.

strands.strand: Technical knowledge: mechanical systems (gears, pulleys, cams, levers, linkages)
strands.gapNote: No D&T app explores gears, pulleys, cams and linkages to create movement; the science circuit app does not cover mechanics.

suggestedApps.title: Gear and Pulley Playground
suggestedApps.concept: Connect gears and pulleys of different sizes and watch live ratios change speed and direction, then design a mechanism for a given job.

suggestedApps.title: Cam Toy Designer
suggestedApps.concept: Pick a cam shape (snail, pear, eccentric) and see the up-down motion it makes a follower produce, then design an automaton with the right movement.

strands.strand: Technical knowledge: electrical systems (simple circuits, switches, bulbs, buzzers, motors)
strands.gapNote: No D&T-purposed app embeds circuits into a product context; science-circuit-builder.html teaches circuits as physics, not as a D&T system in a product.

suggestedApps.title: Power Your Product
suggestedApps.concept: Wire a switch, bulb, buzzer or motor into a product the class designs (a torch, a alarm), testing the circuit then placing it inside the product.

strands.strand: Cooking and nutrition (principles of a healthy and varied diet; preparing dishes; seasonality and food sourcing)
strands.gapNote: No app teaches healthy balanced diets, savoury dish preparation and food provenance at KS2.

suggestedApps.title: Balance the Plate
suggestedApps.concept: Drag foods onto an Eatwell-style plate and get live feedback on balance, then plan and sequence a simple savoury dish using a range of techniques.

suggestedApps.title: Seasonal and Sourced
suggestedApps.concept: A calendar-and-map tool where pupils discover which foods are in season and how they are grown, caught or reared before designing a seasonal recipe.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Design (user-centred design, briefs and specifications, exploring contexts, communicating with sketching, modelling and CAD)
strands.gapNote: No app supports KS3 user-centred briefs, specifications and communication of design intent.

suggestedApps.title: From Context to Spec
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils interrogate a design context, identify user needs, write a measurable specification, then sketch and annotate concepts that meet each spec point.

suggestedApps.title: CAD Concept Studio
suggestedApps.concept: A 3D modelling sandbox to develop a concept, change parameters and render views, showing how CAD supports iteration and communication.

strands.strand: Make (planning, selecting from wider materials and components, precise tools, techniques and processes)
strands.gapNote: No app models KS3 production planning, material selection and accurate manufacturing techniques.

suggestedApps.title: Production Plan Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: Sequence the manufacture of a product, allocate tools, materials and quality checks, then run it and see where bottlenecks or errors occur.

strands.strand: Evaluate (testing and critiquing against specification; investigating past and present design and technology)
strands.gapNote: No app supports structured KS3 evaluation, testing and analysis of existing products.

suggestedApps.title: Critique the Product
suggestedApps.concept: Dissect a real product on screen, evaluate it against a spec, ergonomics and sustainability, then propose evidence-based improvements.

strands.strand: Technical knowledge: materials and their properties (timbers, metals, polymers, textiles, smart and modern materials)
strands.gapNote: No app teaches categories of materials, their working properties and selection for purpose.

suggestedApps.title: Material Selector
suggestedApps.concept: Pick a product requirement and compare timbers, metals, polymers and smart materials on properties like strength, density and cost to justify a choice.

strands.strand: Technical knowledge: mechanical and electronic systems (mechanisms, inputs-process-outputs, programmable components)
strands.gapNote: No app links mechanical and electronic systems with inputs, processes and outputs at KS3; science apps do not frame this as systems design.

suggestedApps.title: Systems Block Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Wire input, process and output blocks (sensor to microcontroller to motor or LED) into a working system simulation and tweak the logic to change behaviour.

suggestedApps.title: Mechanism Designer
suggestedApps.concept: Combine gears, cams and linkages to convert one type of motion into another for a real design problem, with live motion playback.

strands.strand: Cooking and nutrition (cook a repertoire of savoury dishes; understand source, seasonality and characteristics of ingredients; healthy diet)
strands.gapNote: No app supports KS3 nutrition, dish development and ingredient knowledge.

suggestedApps.title: Nutrient Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Build a meal and watch macro and micronutrient totals update live against dietary guidelines, then adapt the recipe to hit a nutritional target.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (GCSE Design and Technology and GCSE Food Preparation and Nutrition)

strands.strand: Core technical principles (materials and their properties, energy generation and storage, modern and smart materials, systems)
strands.gapNote: No app covers GCSE core technical principles across material categories and energy.

suggestedApps.title: Smart Material Showcase
suggestedApps.concept: Interact with shape memory alloys, thermochromics and composites by changing temperature, stress or current and seeing the material respond, then match each to a product use.

strands.strand: Specialist technical principles (forces, stresses, structural reinforcement, mechanical devices, electronic systems and programming)
strands.gapNote: No app models GCSE specialist principles such as forces, mechanical devices or electronic system design.

suggestedApps.title: Stress and Reinforcement Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Apply tension, compression, shear and torsion to a component and trial reinforcement methods (webbing, lamination, triangulation) to resist failure.

suggestedApps.title: Electronic System Designer
suggestedApps.concept: Build a sensor-driven electronic circuit with a programmable microcontroller, write simple flowchart logic and simulate the output behaviour.

strands.strand: Designing and making principles (iterative design, user-centred design, ergonomics, prototyping, tolerances, quality control)
strands.gapNote: No app supports the GCSE iterative design and prototyping process or tolerances and QC.

suggestedApps.title: Iterative Design Loop
suggestedApps.concept: Run a full design cycle on screen: explore, create, evaluate, refine, with version history and user feedback feeding each iteration.

strands.strand: Industry, manufacture and sustainability (scales of production, CAD/CAM, lifecycle, the 6 Rs, social and environmental impact)
strands.gapNote: No app addresses manufacturing scales, CAD/CAM or sustainability and the 6 Rs.

suggestedApps.title: Production Scale Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: Choose one-off, batch or mass production for a product and compare cost, time, tooling and CAD/CAM use, then assess the lifecycle and 6 Rs impact.

strands.strand: Food preparation and cooking skills (techniques, the science of cooking, food safety)
strands.gapNote: No app covers GCSE Food Prep cooking techniques or the science of heat transfer and functional properties.

suggestedApps.title: Cooking Science Bench
suggestedApps.concept: Manipulate heat, time and ingredients to see denaturation, gelatinisation, aeration and caramelisation happen, then explain each functional change.

strands.strand: Nutrition, diet and food provenance (nutrients, energy needs, diet-related health, food choice, sourcing and processing)
strands.gapNote: No app supports GCSE nutrition science, diet-related health or food provenance.

suggestedApps.title: Diet Profiler
suggestedApps.concept: Build a day of meals for a chosen profile (athlete, toddler, diabetic) and analyse energy and nutrient balance against requirements, then adjust to meet needs.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (A-level Design and Technology: Product Design / Fashion and Textiles)

strands.strand: Materials, components and systems (advanced material properties, selection, smart/modern materials, electronic and mechanical systems)
strands.gapNote: No app covers A-level depth on advanced materials selection and systems.

suggestedApps.title: Advanced Material Matrix
suggestedApps.concept: Compare advanced materials and composites on a multi-axis property chart, run a weighted selection matrix for a brief and justify the optimal choice.

strands.strand: Performance characteristics, forces and structural analysis (stress, strain, factors of safety, mechanical advantage)
strands.gapNote: No app supports quantitative structural and mechanical analysis at A-level.

suggestedApps.title: Stress-Strain Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Load virtual specimens, plot live stress-strain curves, read off yield and ultimate strength and calculate factors of safety for a design decision.

strands.strand: Design theory, ergonomics and human factors (anthropometrics, percentiles, inclusive and user-centred design)
strands.gapNote: No app teaches anthropometric data, percentiles and inclusive design at A-level.

suggestedApps.title: Anthropometrics Explorer
suggestedApps.concept: Set a target percentile range and watch product dimensions auto-adjust to fit, testing inclusive design against real anthropometric datasets.

strands.strand: Manufacturing, processes and CAD/CAM (industrial processes, automation, scales of production, digital manufacture)
strands.gapNote: No app covers advanced manufacturing processes, automation and CAD/CAM at A-level.

suggestedApps.title: Process Chooser Pro
suggestedApps.concept: Match a component to the right industrial process (injection moulding, CNC, additive) by trialling each on cost, volume, tolerance and finish, then justify the choice.

strands.strand: Sustainability, lifecycle and wider impacts (lifecycle analysis, circular economy, social, ethical, legal and environmental responsibility)
strands.gapNote: No app supports A-level lifecycle analysis, circular economy or wider responsibilities.

suggestedApps.title: Lifecycle Analyst
suggestedApps.concept: Run a full lifecycle assessment of a product from extraction to disposal, model circular-economy interventions and see the carbon and impact totals shift.

strands.strand: Design and make project / NEA process (briefs, research, iterative development, testing, evaluation and commercial viability)
strands.gapNote: No app scaffolds the A-level NEA iterative design and evaluation process.

suggestedApps.title: NEA Design Journey
suggestedApps.concept: A structured project workspace that guides exploration, client research, iterative prototyping, testing and a commercial-viability evaluation with evidence capture at each stage.

summary.headline: Design and Technology has zero app coverage across every key stage and strand, with the most acute gaps in the hands-on technical strands (mechanisms, structures, electrical/electronic systems and cooking and nutrition) where interactive teacher demos would have the greatest impact.

subject: Physical Education
sourceNotes: Strands derived from the statutory DfE National Curriculum in England: Physical Education programmes of study (gov.uk, fetched 29/05/2026) for KS1-KS4, the EYFS statutory framework Physical Development area of learning (gross and fine motor), and typical GCSE PE / A-level PE exam-board subject content for KS4/KS5. No local curriculum summary file exists for this subject; all coverage is none as the trust currently holds zero PE apps (verified against the 209-file apps/ directory).

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (EYFS framework - Physical Development prime area)

strands.strand: Gross motor skills (moving, balancing, coordination)
strands.gapNote: No app supports fundamental movement, balance or spatial awareness for Reception.

suggestedApps.title: Movement Mat
suggestedApps.concept: Projected animated movement cards (hop, skip, balance, crawl) the class follows in real time, with a teacher dial to sequence a mini circuit.

suggestedApps.title: Balance Beam Buddies
suggestedApps.concept: Whiteboard-controlled avatar that wobbles when children copy poses, teaching static and dynamic balance through live mirroring.

strands.strand: Fine motor skills and hand control
strands.gapNote: Nothing covers grip, manipulation or tool/equipment control linked to physical play.

suggestedApps.title: Grip and Grab
suggestedApps.concept: Touchscreen drag tasks (threading, pinching, tracing) projected for modelling correct pencil and tool grip before active sessions.

strands.strand: Healthy bodies and active play
strands.gapNote: No app introduces being active, why bodies feel different after movement, or healthy routines.

suggestedApps.title: My Busy Body
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive avatar whose heartbeat and breathing speed up as the class does actions, prompting talk about what exercise does to us.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Fundamental movement skills (run, jump, throw, catch)
strands.gapNote: No app models or assesses core locomotor and object-control skills.

suggestedApps.title: Throw and Catch Coach
suggestedApps.concept: Slow-motion technique animation with a tap-to-pause breakdown of the throw/catch action, plus a class self-assessment thumbs grid.

suggestedApps.title: Skill Spinner
suggestedApps.concept: Projected wheel that generates a fundamental skill plus a fun challenge so the teacher can run quick rotation stations.

strands.strand: Balance, agility and coordination
strands.gapNote: Nothing supports developing or demonstrating balance and coordination.

suggestedApps.title: Agility Ladder Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Animated footwork ladder patterns the teacher projects and speeds up, with arrows showing where feet go for each agility drill.

strands.strand: Team games: simple attacking and defending
strands.gapNote: No app introduces basic tactics, space and rules of simple games.

suggestedApps.title: Space Invaders PE
suggestedApps.concept: Top-down pitch where children drag attacker/defender counters to discover finding space and marking, with a teacher reveal of best moves.

strands.strand: Dance and movement to music
strands.gapNote: No app supports performing simple movement patterns or rhythm work.

suggestedApps.title: Move to the Beat
suggestedApps.concept: Beat-synced movement prompts on the board (stretch, turn, jump) building a class dance phrase the children remember and perform.

strands.strand: Healthy, active lifestyles
strands.gapNote: Nothing connects movement to health, warm-ups or how the body responds to exercise at KS1.

suggestedApps.title: Warm-Up Wizard
suggestedApps.concept: Animated character leading a pulse-raiser/stretch routine with a slider the class uses to feel heart-rate change before and after.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (includes statutory swimming requirement at KS1 or KS2)

strands.strand: Invasion games: tactics, attacking and defending
strands.gapNote: No app teaches positioning, space and tactics for football, hockey, netball or basketball.

suggestedApps.title: Tactics Board Live
suggestedApps.concept: Drag-and-drop pitch for netball/football where pupils plan an attack, then the app animates the play to test if it works.

suggestedApps.title: Find the Space
suggestedApps.concept: Freeze-frame match images where the class taps the best passing option, with instant reveal and reasoning.

strands.strand: Net/wall and striking/fielding games
strands.gapNote: Nothing covers tennis, badminton, cricket or rounders skills, rules or fielding tactics.

suggestedApps.title: Field the Ball
suggestedApps.concept: Striking/fielding diamond where pupils position fielders against different hits and see the percentage of balls stopped.

strands.strand: Gymnastics: sequences, flexibility and strength
strands.gapNote: No app supports building and refining movement sequences or apparatus work.

suggestedApps.title: Sequence Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Drag gymnastics shapes/rolls/jumps into a timeline to compose a balanced routine, with auto feedback on variety and flow.

strands.strand: Dance: complex patterns and composition
strands.gapNote: Nothing supports choreographing or evaluating more complex dance.

suggestedApps.title: Choreo Canvas
suggestedApps.concept: Timeline tool to arrange motifs, levels and formations, previewing the routine with stick-figure dancers for class critique.

strands.strand: Athletics: running, jumping and throwing for performance
strands.gapNote: No app develops or measures sprint, distance, jump or throw technique and progress.

suggestedApps.title: Athletics Arena
suggestedApps.concept: Technique animations plus a class results board pupils enter times/distances into, auto-charting personal bests.

strands.strand: Outdoor and adventurous activity (problem solving)
strands.gapNote: No app supports orienteering, teamwork challenges or problem solving.

suggestedApps.title: Orienteering Quest
suggestedApps.concept: Projected map with control points where teams plan the fastest route and the app times decisions and reveals the optimal path.

strands.strand: Swimming and water safety (statutory 25m, strokes, self-rescue)
strands.gapNote: Nothing supports stroke technique, water safety or tracking the 25m statutory outcome.

suggestedApps.title: Stroke Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Side-on animations of front crawl/breaststroke with a fault-finder where pupils spot and fix technique errors.

suggestedApps.title: Water Safety Scenarios
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive open-water situations where the class chooses safe self-rescue actions and sees the outcome.

strands.strand: Evaluating performance and improvement
strands.gapNote: No app supports comparing own/others performance to personal bests and setting next steps.

suggestedApps.title: Coach's Eye
suggestedApps.concept: Side-by-side clips/diagrams pupils annotate with strengths and one improvement, building peer-assessment habits.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory

strands.strand: Competitive games: advanced tactics and strategy
strands.gapNote: No app develops decision-making, formations or game strategy across invasion/net/striking games.

suggestedApps.title: Game Plan Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive formation board where students set up a team system, then run animated scenarios to test the tactic against an opponent.

strands.strand: Refining techniques and skilled performance
strands.gapNote: Nothing supports analysing and refining technique across a range of activities.

suggestedApps.title: Technique Analyser
suggestedApps.concept: Phase-by-phase models of skills (serve, layup, vault) with a tap-to-mark checklist the class uses to coach a volunteer.

strands.strand: Dance and gymnastics: advanced composition
strands.gapNote: No app supports more demanding choreography, apparatus sequences or performance evaluation.

suggestedApps.title: Composition Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Drag motifs, canon, unison and dynamics onto a timeline and preview the staged piece for whole-class refinement.

strands.strand: Outdoor and adventurous activities (teamwork, problem solving)
strands.gapNote: Nothing supports OAA leadership, navigation or group problem solving at KS3.

suggestedApps.title: Expedition Planner
suggestedApps.concept: Teams plan a route under time/weather/equipment constraints; the app simulates consequences of their choices.

strands.strand: Fitness, health and active lifestyles
strands.gapNote: No app covers components of fitness, training types or healthy lifestyle behaviours.

suggestedApps.title: Fitness Components Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive matcher linking sports to fitness components, then build a circuit and see which components it trains.

suggestedApps.title: Heart Rate Modeller
suggestedApps.concept: Adjust intensity sliders to see heart rate, training zones and recovery animate in real time.

strands.strand: Analysing and evaluating performance
strands.gapNote: No app supports structured analysis to identify strengths and improvement priorities.

suggestedApps.title: Performance Profiler
suggestedApps.concept: Radar-chart tool where students rate a performance on key criteria and the app prioritises one development target.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (core PE); GCSE PE is exam-board defined (AQA/Edexcel/OCR/Eduqas)

strands.strand: Applied anatomy and physiology
strands.gapNote: No app covers musculoskeletal, cardiorespiratory and energy systems for GCSE PE.

suggestedApps.title: Body in Motion
suggestedApps.concept: Clickable muscle and skeleton model showing antagonistic pairs and joint actions when students animate a chosen movement.

suggestedApps.title: Cardio-Respiratory Walkthrough
suggestedApps.concept: Animated gas exchange and blood flow that responds to an exercise-intensity slider, with labelled checkpoints.

strands.strand: Movement analysis (levers, planes and axes)
strands.gapNote: Nothing supports lever systems, planes/axes or mechanical advantage analysis.

suggestedApps.title: Lever Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Drag the fulcrum, load and effort on a limb diagram to classify lever systems and compute mechanical advantage live.

strands.strand: Physical training and fitness testing
strands.gapNote: No app covers principles of training, methods or fitness test protocols and data.

suggestedApps.title: Training Programme Designer
suggestedApps.concept: Build a periodised plan applying SPORT/FITT, with the app flagging overtraining and progression errors.

suggestedApps.title: Fitness Test Bench
suggestedApps.concept: Run virtual fitness tests, enter results and auto-rate against normative data tables.

strands.strand: Sport psychology
strands.gapNote: Nothing covers skill classification, goal setting, arousal or guidance/feedback.

suggestedApps.title: Arousal and Performance
suggestedApps.concept: Drag the inverted-U curve for different skills/athletes to predict optimal arousal, with worked exam-style scenarios.

strands.strand: Socio-cultural influences in sport
strands.gapNote: No app covers participation, commercialisation, ethics or engagement patterns.

suggestedApps.title: Sport and Society Explorer
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive data dashboard on participation by group, with toggles linking barriers to strategies that raise engagement.

strands.strand: Health, fitness and well-being
strands.gapNote: Nothing covers diet, energy balance, sedentary lifestyle consequences for GCSE.

suggestedApps.title: Energy Balance Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: Adjust diet and activity inputs to model energy balance and long-term health outcomes, with macronutrient breakdown.

strands.strand: Practical performance and personal exercise programme
strands.gapNote: No app supports practical assessment criteria or the coursework PEP analysis cycle.

suggestedApps.title: PEP Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Guided coursework scaffold that plans, logs and analyses a personal exercise programme with auto-generated progress graphs.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined (A-level PE: AQA/OCR/Edexcel); non-statutory

strands.strand: Exercise physiology and energy systems
strands.gapNote: No app covers ATP-PC/glycolytic/aerobic systems, oxygen uptake or recovery at A-level depth.

suggestedApps.title: Energy Systems Continuum
suggestedApps.concept: Adjust intensity and duration to see which energy system dominates, animating fuel use, fatigue and EPOC recovery.

strands.strand: Biomechanics
strands.gapNote: Nothing covers force, motion, projectile, angular motion or fluid mechanics.

suggestedApps.title: Biomechanics Sandbox
suggestedApps.concept: Launch a projectile and vary angle, speed and height while the app plots trajectory and explains the governing factors.

strands.strand: Skill acquisition and motor learning
strands.gapNote: No app covers learning theories, practice/feedback types or information processing models.

suggestedApps.title: Information Processing Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive Whiting/Welford model where students route a sporting example through each stage and test reaction-time effects.

strands.strand: Sport psychology (advanced)
strands.gapNote: Nothing covers attribution, leadership, group dynamics or stress management at A-level.

suggestedApps.title: Attribution Modeller
suggestedApps.concept: Place outcomes on the Weiner locus/stability grid and see predicted effects on confidence and motivation.

strands.strand: Sport and society and contemporary issues
strands.gapNote: No app covers the development of sport, globalisation, commercialisation or technology.

suggestedApps.title: Sport Through Time
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive timeline from pre-industrial to global sport, toggling social factors to show how participation evolved.

strands.strand: Performance analysis and improvement (coursework/EAPI)
strands.gapNote: Nothing supports the analytical evaluation and programme of improvement coursework cycle.

suggestedApps.title: EAPI Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Structured analysis tool to identify a performance weakness, justify causes against theory and build an improvement programme.

summary.headline: Physical Education has zero coverage across all 37 strands from EYFS to KS5; the most urgent gaps are statutory primary PE (fundamental movement, games tactics and the statutory swimming/water-safety outcome) and GCSE/A-level theory (anatomy and physiology, training and biomechanics).

subject: Citizenship
sourceNotes: Statutory programme of study confirmed via gov.uk/legislation.gov.uk web search (National Curriculum in England, KS3-4 framework, 2014, statutory from Sept 2014; cuts to human rights/local democracy content, added personal finance/economic understanding). KS1-2 mapped from the QCA/QCDA non-statutory citizenship framework and typical PSHE-association schemes of work (web search confirmed citizenship is non-statutory at primary, often delivered through PSHE). KS5 mapped from typical A-level/Citizenship Studies and enrichment provision (no single statutory A-level NC). Existing-app cross-reference from a directory listing of /apps (209 files); only one tangentially related file found (re-rights-responsibilities.html, an RE/ethics scenario tool).

keyStages.statutoryNote: non-statutory (citizenship is non-statutory at primary; commonly delivered through the PSHE association framework and the QCA citizenship guidance)

strands.strand: Rules, fairness and taking part (classroom and community)
strands.gapNote: No app on why we have rules, fairness, turn-taking or class decision-making for infants.

suggestedApps.title: Build Our Class Rules
suggestedApps.concept: Class drags suggested rules onto a board, votes with a live tally, and sees instantly how a 'no rule' classroom descends into chaos via an animated playground simulation.

suggestedApps.title: Fair Share Sorter
suggestedApps.concept: Teacher projects scenarios (sweets, turns on a bike) and pupils tap 'fair' or 'unfair', then drag to make each one fair, with reasoning prompts.

strands.strand: Belonging: me, my family and my community
strands.gapNote: Nothing on identity, families and the people who help us in the local community.

suggestedApps.title: Who Helps Us?
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive village map where tapping a building (school, surgery, fire station) reveals the helper, what they do, and a 'thank you' action the class designs.

strands.strand: Looking after our world (caring for places and living things)
strands.gapNote: No app on responsibility for shared spaces, litter, recycling or caring for animals/plants.

suggestedApps.title: Tidy Town
suggestedApps.concept: A drag-and-sort recycling game on a shared town scene where the class collectively cleans a park and watches a live 'happiness meter' rise.

strands.strand: Money: what it is and choices we make
strands.gapNote: No early-finance app on coins, needs vs wants or simple spending choices.

suggestedApps.title: Needs or Wants?
suggestedApps.concept: Items fly across the screen and the class sorts them into needs vs wants, then spends a fixed pot of coins in a shop with instant change feedback.

strands.strand: Right and wrong: simple choices and consequences
strands.gapNote: No app modelling simple moral choices and their consequences for infants.

suggestedApps.title: What Happens Next?
suggestedApps.concept: Branching animated stories where the class votes on a character's choice and watches the consequence play out, then discusses a kinder option.

keyStages.statutoryNote: non-statutory (citizenship is non-statutory at primary; commonly delivered through PSHE and the QCA citizenship framework)

strands.strand: Democracy, voting and how decisions are made
strands.gapNote: No app introducing voting, school councils or how group decisions are made democratically.

suggestedApps.title: Run a School Election
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils create manifesto cards, the class casts secret ballots, and a live results board shows first-past-the-post in action with a turnout counter.

suggestedApps.title: How a Decision Is Made
suggestedApps.concept: A flow-board where a class proposal travels from idea to council vote to action, with the class moving it through each stage and seeing where it can be blocked.

strands.strand: Rules, laws and why society needs them
strands.gapNote: No app distinguishing rules from laws or exploring why laws exist and who makes them.

suggestedApps.title: Rule or Law?
suggestedApps.concept: A live sorter where pupils classify statements as a school rule, a law, or neither, then see the consequence ladder for breaking each.

strands.strand: Rights, responsibilities and the rights of the child
strands.gapNote: re-rights-responsibilities.html is an RE/ethics scenario balancer, not a primary citizenship tool on the UN rights of the child.

suggestedApps.title: Rights of the Child Explorer
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive carousel of UNCRC rights where pupils match each right to a real classroom scenario and balance it against a matching responsibility.

strands.strand: Communities, diversity and identity in the UK
strands.gapNote: No app exploring the diverse communities, cultures and identities that make up modern Britain.

suggestedApps.title: Our Diverse Community
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive 'town' the class populates with people of different backgrounds, faiths and jobs, revealing how diversity strengthens a community via mini-stories.

strands.strand: Money management and being a sensible consumer
strands.gapNote: No app on budgeting pocket money, saving, value for money or being a careful consumer.

suggestedApps.title: Budget the Class Trip
suggestedApps.concept: The class plans a day out within a fixed budget, dragging activities and snacks into a live-totalling planner that flags overspend.

strands.strand: Active citizenship: making a difference locally
strands.gapNote: No app on identifying a local issue and planning a campaign or social action project.

suggestedApps.title: Campaign Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Pupils pick a local issue, then assemble a campaign (poster, petition, who to write to) on a planning board that simulates how much impact each action adds.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (National Curriculum foundation subject, programme of study from Sept 2014)

strands.strand: UK government, Parliament and the democratic system
strands.gapNote: No app on the structure of UK government, Parliament, the role of the monarch, the PM and Cabinet.

suggestedApps.title: How Parliament Works
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive Westminster diagram where students move a bill through Commons, Lords and Royal Assent, hitting amendments and votes at each live stage.

suggestedApps.title: Who Holds Power?
suggestedApps.concept: A drag-and-connect map of monarch, PM, Cabinet, MPs and civil service where students link each role to its real powers and see the checks and balances light up.

strands.strand: Elections, voting systems and political participation
strands.gapNote: No app on how elections work, first-past-the-post vs other systems, or ways citizens participate.

suggestedApps.title: Election Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Students run the same vote through FPTP, AV and PR side by side and watch how the same ballots produce different winners and seat shares.

strands.strand: The law, justice system and how laws are made and enforced
strands.gapNote: No app on the legal system, courts, civil vs criminal law, the police or the justice process.

suggestedApps.title: Courtroom Pathways
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive flowchart taking a case from arrest through charge, court and sentence, where students decide civil vs criminal and see where each route leads.

suggestedApps.title: Make a Law
suggestedApps.concept: A simulation where the class drafts a law, debates it, and watches it pass through the legislative stages with public-opinion and lobbying pressures.

strands.strand: Rights, liberties, responsibilities and the rule of law
strands.gapNote: re-rights-responsibilities.html touches rights vs responsibilities but is an RE scenario tool, not a KS3 citizenship treatment of liberties and the rule of law.

suggestedApps.title: Balance of Liberties
suggestedApps.concept: Students adjust sliders (free speech vs harm, privacy vs security) on real case studies and see the trade-offs and legal limits update live.

strands.strand: Local and devolved government and the role of the citizen
strands.gapNote: No app on local councils, devolved administrations, public services and how citizens engage.

suggestedApps.title: Run the Council
suggestedApps.concept: Students allocate a council budget across services, then face the consequences (potholes, school cuts, public reaction) in a live what-if dashboard.

strands.strand: Personal finance: money, budgeting, credit and risk
strands.gapNote: No app on income, budgeting, saving, borrowing, interest, debt risk and financial planning at KS3.

suggestedApps.title: Money Months
suggestedApps.concept: Students manage a young person's monthly budget through random life events, watching savings, debt and interest compound across a year.

suggestedApps.title: The Cost of Credit
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive borrowing simulator comparing loans, credit cards and BNPL where students see total repayable balloon as APR and term change.

strands.strand: The UK's role in the wider world and global issues
strands.gapNote: No app on the UK's relationship with Europe, the Commonwealth, the UN or global citizenship.

suggestedApps.title: Global Connections Map
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive world map where students explore the UK's membership of the UN, NATO and Commonwealth and trace how a global issue reaches local lives.

strands.strand: Identity, diversity and living together in the UK
strands.gapNote: No app on the diverse national, regional, religious and ethnic identities of the UK and mutual respect.

suggestedApps.title: What Makes Us British?
suggestedApps.concept: A live word-cloud and sorting activity where students build a shared picture of identity and diversity, testing assumptions against real census data.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (National Curriculum foundation subject KS4; also offered as GCSE Citizenship Studies, exam-board defined)

strands.strand: Parliamentary democracy and the British political system
strands.gapNote: No app on the key elements of the constitution, power, the executive, legislature and judiciary at KS4.

suggestedApps.title: Power Map of Britain
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive constitution diagram where students trace how power flows and is checked between Crown, Parliament, government and courts, stress-tested with real scenarios.

strands.strand: Elections, political parties and citizen participation
strands.gapNote: No app on parties, manifestos, pressure groups, campaigning and active participation at GCSE level.

suggestedApps.title: Win the Marginal
suggestedApps.concept: Students run a campaign in a marginal seat, spending limited resources on tactics and watching live polling shift in response.

strands.strand: The legal system, justice and the rights of citizens
strands.gapNote: No app on the operation of justice, courts, legal rights, employment and consumer law at KS4.

suggestedApps.title: Know Your Rights
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive case-tree on consumer, employment and police-encounter rights where students choose actions and see the legal outcome and remedy.

strands.strand: Human rights, equality and the law (Equality Act, ECHR)
strands.gapNote: re-rights-responsibilities.html is an RE/ethics balancer; nothing covers the Equality Act, protected characteristics or the ECHR at KS4.

suggestedApps.title: Equality Act in Action
suggestedApps.concept: Students judge real-style discrimination scenarios against the nine protected characteristics and see whether the law was breached and what redress applies.

strands.strand: Active citizenship and social action projects
strands.gapNote: No app to plan, run and evaluate a citizenship social action campaign (a core GCSE requirement).

suggestedApps.title: Action Project Planner
suggestedApps.concept: A live project board guiding students from issue to advocacy method to measurable impact, modelling stakeholder reactions as they choose tactics.

strands.strand: Personal finance, taxation and public money
strands.gapNote: No app on income tax, NI, VAT, the public purse, where tax goes and personal financial planning at KS4.

suggestedApps.title: Where Your Tax Goes
suggestedApps.concept: Students set tax rates and a national budget and watch services, deficit and public reaction respond live, linking the payslip to public spending.

suggestedApps.title: First Payslip Decoder
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive payslip where students toggle income tax, NI, pension and student loan and see take-home pay and lifetime impact update.

strands.strand: The media, public debate and being a critical citizen
strands.gapNote: No app on the role of a free press, misinformation, bias and evaluating sources as a citizen.

suggestedApps.title: Spot the Spin
suggestedApps.concept: Students compare how outlets report the same event, tagging bias, framing and missing context, with a live 'reliability score' they justify.

strands.strand: The UK, Europe and the international community
strands.gapNote: No app on the UK's post-Brexit relationship with Europe, the UN, NATO and international law at KS4.

suggestedApps.title: Britain in the World
suggestedApps.concept: An interactive treaty-and-membership board where students manage the UK's international ties and see trade, security and influence trade-offs.

keyStages.statutoryNote: exam-board defined / non-statutory (no statutory NC at KS5; A-level Citizenship and post-16 enrichment, plus tutorial-based civic and financial education)

strands.strand: Politics, power and democratic theory
strands.gapNote: No app on democratic models, ideologies, sovereignty and the distribution of power for post-16 study.

suggestedApps.title: Ideology Compass
suggestedApps.concept: Students place real policies on a political-spectrum grid and see how liberal, conservative and socialist traditions would each respond, with live debate prompts.

strands.strand: Citizen participation, campaigning and advocacy
strands.gapNote: No app on advanced advocacy, lobbying, direct action and measuring civic impact at KS5.

suggestedApps.title: Pressure Group Strategist
suggestedApps.concept: Students run a pressure-group campaign choosing insider vs outsider tactics and watch influence, media coverage and policy change respond in real time.

strands.strand: Law, rights and global justice
strands.gapNote: No app on human rights frameworks, international law and global justice issues for post-16 learners.

suggestedApps.title: Rights on Trial
suggestedApps.concept: Students argue landmark human-rights cases, weighing competing rights and seeing how courts balanced them, with a live verdict-prediction model.

strands.strand: Financial capability for adult life
strands.gapNote: No app on student finance, tax, pensions, mortgages, credit scores and major life financial decisions for 16-19.

suggestedApps.title: Adult Life Finance Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: Students steer a young adult through university, first job, renting and a mortgage, watching credit score, debt and net worth evolve across a decade.

strands.strand: Global citizenship and contemporary world issues
strands.gapNote: No app on sustainability, migration, conflict and the citizen's role in global challenges at KS5.

suggestedApps.title: Global Challenge Room
suggestedApps.concept: Teams negotiate a global problem (climate, migration) as competing nations, seeing how national interest and cooperation reshape the outcome live.

summary.headline: Citizenship is almost entirely uncovered (0 of 32 strands fully covered); the only touchpoint is an RE rights/responsibilities tool, leaving the statutory KS3-KS4 core (government and democracy, law and justice, and personal finance) as the biggest gap.

subject: PSHE, RSHE and Behaviour/Pastoral
sourceNotes: DfE statutory guidance "Relationships Education, RSE and Health Education" (2019, updated for Sept 2026) verified via gov.uk web search for primary Relationships Education and secondary RSE/Health Education statutory themes (families, caring friendships, respectful relationships, online and media, being safe, mental wellbeing, internet safety and harms, physical health and fitness, healthy eating, drugs/alcohol/tobacco, health and prevention, basic first aid, changing adolescent body). Non-statutory PSHE breadth (Living in the Wider World: economic wellbeing/careers, rights and responsibilities) drawn from PSHE Association programme of study and model knowledge. EYFS strands from the EYFS statutory framework (Personal, Social and Emotional Development). Behaviour and Pastoral strand set is WeST-defined (self-regulation, relationships and conflict, resilience and growth mindset, restorative practice, belonging and attendance, online/digital wellbeing). Existing apps read from /tmp/subject_apps.json key "PSHE-RSHE-Behaviour".

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory (EYFS framework: Personal, Social and Emotional Development)

strands.strand: Self-regulation and managing feelings
strands.gapNote: Emotion Thermometer and Zones of Regulation exist but are pitched at older readers and assume independent reading; nothing EYFS-pitched with pictures/colours.

suggestedApps.title: Feelings Faces Match
suggestedApps.concept: Big animated faces show emotions; children drag a matching colour or calming action (deep breath, ask for help) onto each face, teacher-projected with audio prompts.

suggestedApps.title: Calm-Down Corner Builder
suggestedApps.concept: Class taps to assemble a calm-down toolkit (breathing star, squeeze, count to five) that animates on screen so the teacher can model each strategy live.

strands.strand: Making relationships and playing with others
strands.gapNote: No EYFS app on sharing, turn-taking and joining in play.

suggestedApps.title: Sharing the Sandpit
suggestedApps.concept: Animated scenario where two characters both want one toy; class votes on what to do next and sees each choice play out (turn-taking, asking, sharing).

strands.strand: Self-confidence, independence and trying new things
strands.gapNote: Growth Mindset Reframer touches perseverance but uses written statements unsuitable for EYFS.

suggestedApps.title: The Try-Again Tree
suggestedApps.concept: Each time the class taps to have a go at a tricky task the tree grows a leaf; models that effort and mistakes help us grow, fully visual.

strands.strand: Sense of belonging, routines and being safe
strands.gapNote: No EYFS app on classroom belonging, daily routines or knowing safe adults.

suggestedApps.title: Our Day Picture Timeline
suggestedApps.concept: Drag-and-drop visual timetable the class orders together each morning to build routine, security and belonging.

suggestedApps.title: My Safe Grown-Ups
suggestedApps.concept: Children populate a simple hand or circle with pictures of trusted adults to talk to; teacher-led discussion of who helps us.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory Relationships Education and Health Education; PSHE non-statutory

strands.strand: Families and people who care for me
strands.gapNote: No app on different family structures and who cares for us at KS1.

suggestedApps.title: All Our Families
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive gallery of varied family set-ups; class sorts what families have in common (love, care, keeping safe) by dragging cards onto a shared chart.

strands.strand: Caring friendships and getting on/falling out
strands.gapNote: Friendship Network Mapper and Conflict Resolution Flowchart exist but read older; nothing simple on what makes a good friend at KS1.

suggestedApps.title: Good Friend or Not?
suggestedApps.concept: Animated short scenes; class signals thumbs up/down and the app reveals why an action is kind or unkind, building a shared friendship charter.

strands.strand: Recognising and naming feelings
strands.gapNote: Emotion Thermometer covers intensity but is text-heavy; KS1 needs a vocabulary-building tool.

suggestedApps.title: Feelings Word Wall
suggestedApps.concept: Tap a face to grow a word bank of feelings vocabulary; class matches a feeling to a 'what could help' card, projected and discussed.

strands.strand: Being safe, privacy and asking for help
strands.gapNote: No KS1 app on the underwear rule, secrets vs surprises, or who/how to tell.

suggestedApps.title: Tell a Trusted Adult
suggestedApps.concept: Branching what-would-you-do scenes (a worry, a secret that feels wrong); class chooses to keep it in or tell, and sees the relief of telling.

strands.strand: Healthy bodies: hygiene, sleep, healthy eating and physical activity
strands.gapNote: No KS1 health-education app on basic hygiene, sleep or healthy eating.

suggestedApps.title: Build a Healthy Day
suggestedApps.concept: Drag activities (teeth-brushing, fruit, play, sleep) onto a 24-hour wheel; the wheel lights up green as a balanced healthy day is built.

strands.strand: Internet and screens: safe and kind online
strands.gapNote: No KS1 app introducing basic online safety and telling an adult about online worries.

suggestedApps.title: Smart Tap Tablet
suggestedApps.concept: Simulated tablet shows pop-ups and messages; class decides tap or stop and tell, learning the first rules of staying safe online.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory Relationships Education and Health Education; PSHE non-statutory

strands.strand: Respectful relationships and treating others fairly
strands.gapNote: Perspective Swap Simulator builds empathy but does not explicitly cover respect, courtesy and stereotypes; no dedicated app.

suggestedApps.title: Respect Detectives
suggestedApps.concept: Class examines classroom and online scenes for respectful vs disrespectful behaviour, builds a respect rule, and tests it against tricky edge cases.

strands.strand: Friendships, conflict and managing fall-outs
Bullying including cyberbullying: recognising and responding
Mental wellbeing, emotions and self-regulation
Internet safety, screen time and being critical online
strands.gapNote: Scenario Navigator touches cyberbullying but nothing on screen-time balance, reliability of online content, or sharing/privacy.

suggestedApps.title: Real or Fake Feed
suggestedApps.concept: A mock social feed the class swipes through, judging whether posts are true, an advert, or unsafe to share, with a running 'critical thinking' score.

suggestedApps.title: Screen-Time Balancer
suggestedApps.concept: Class allocates a day's hours across screens, sleep, activity and friends; the app shows the wellbeing impact of each balance.

strands.strand: Physical health: healthy eating, exercise, sleep, drugs/alcohol awareness
strands.gapNote: No KS2 app on diet, exercise, sleep or first awareness of drugs, alcohol and tobacco.

suggestedApps.title: Fuel Your Body
suggestedApps.concept: Build a balanced plate and weekly activity plan; the on-screen avatar's energy and mood respond to the choices made.

strands.strand: Changing adolescent body and puberty
strands.gapNote: No KS2 app on the statutory puberty/changing body content.

suggestedApps.title: Growing and Changing
suggestedApps.concept: Age-appropriate interactive timeline of physical and emotional puberty changes; class sorts true/false myths and matches changes to self-care.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory RSE and Health Education; PSHE non-statutory

strands.strand: Healthy and respectful relationships, consent and boundaries
strands.gapNote: Restorative Conversation and Perspective Swap support relationship repair but nothing addresses consent, boundaries or healthy intimate relationships.

suggestedApps.title: Boundaries and Consent Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Branching peer scenarios where the class decides what consent looks like, spots pressure and coercion, and practises assertive responses.

strands.strand: Mental health, emotional wellbeing and resilience
Online life: digital wellbeing, harms, pornography and media literacy
strands.gapNote: Scenario Navigator covers bullying but nothing on online harms, misinformation, body image or pornography myths at KS3.

suggestedApps.title: Decode the Feed
suggestedApps.concept: Class dissects influencer/advert/edited-image posts, exposing how media shapes body image and beliefs, scoring their media-literacy calls.

suggestedApps.title: Online Harm Decision Wall
suggestedApps.concept: Live scenarios (sextortion warning signs, grooming red flags, deepfakes) where the class chooses the safest action and sees outcomes.

strands.strand: Physical health: drugs, alcohol, tobacco/vaping and lifestyle
strands.gapNote: No app on risk and effects of drugs, alcohol, smoking and vaping at KS3.

suggestedApps.title: Risk and Reality
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive risk-meter where the class weighs short and long-term effects of substances and rehearses refusal and exit strategies.

strands.strand: Bullying, prejudice, discrimination and being an upstander
strands.gapNote: Scenario Navigator handles bullying but not prejudice/discrimination or active upstander strategies.

suggestedApps.title: Upstander Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: Bystander scenarios with prejudice and discrimination; class chooses how to safely intervene and sees the ripple effect on the group.

strands.strand: Identity, diversity and respect for difference
strands.gapNote: No app on identity, protected characteristics or valuing diversity.

suggestedApps.title: Identity Iceberg
suggestedApps.concept: Class builds an iceberg of visible and hidden identity for a character then their own, challenging stereotypes and assumptions live.

strands.strand: Living in the wider world: money, careers and economic wellbeing
strands.gapNote: No app on budgeting, financial decisions, careers or economic wellbeing.

suggestedApps.title: First Budget Simulator
suggestedApps.concept: Class manages a teenager's monthly money against needs, wants and surprise costs, seeing the consequences of each spending choice.

keyStages.statutoryNote: statutory RSE and Health Education; PSHE non-statutory

strands.strand: Intimate and sexual relationships, consent, contraception and sexual health
strands.gapNote: No app covers the statutory KS4 RSE content on consent, contraception, STIs and healthy intimate relationships.

suggestedApps.title: Consent and Pressure Scenarios
suggestedApps.concept: Realistic branching situations where the class identifies consent, coercion and the law, and rehearses clear communication.

suggestedApps.title: Sexual Health Mythbuster
suggestedApps.concept: Rapid true/false rounds on contraception, STIs and where to get help, with the class building an accurate fact wall.

strands.strand: Recognising abuse, coercive control, harassment and seeking help
strands.gapNote: Scenario Navigator and Restorative Conversation touch conflict but not abuse, coercive control, harassment or safeguarding routes.

suggestedApps.title: Red Flags and Routes to Help
suggestedApps.concept: Class spots warning signs of unhealthy/abusive relationships across scenarios and maps the safest route to support and reporting.

strands.strand: Mental health, stress, self-care and exam wellbeing
strands.gapNote: Zones of Regulation and self-regulation tools help but nothing is pitched at KS4 stress, study pressure or help-seeking.

suggestedApps.title: Stress and Resilience Studio
suggestedApps.concept: Class builds a personalised coping toolkit against realistic exam and life stressors and tests it in a simulated pressure week.

strands.strand: Online safety, digital footprint and media in adulthood
strands.gapNote: Scenario Navigator covers bullying only; nothing on digital footprint, online reputation, sharing images or scams at KS4.

suggestedApps.title: Your Digital Footprint
suggestedApps.concept: Class audits a fictional teen's posts as a future employer/college might, then reverses to clean and protect the footprint.

strands.strand: Substances, risk and personal safety
strands.gapNote: No KS4 app on drugs, alcohol, addiction, risk-taking and personal safety.

suggestedApps.title: Risk Trade-Off Engine
suggestedApps.concept: Class weighs decisions involving substances, peer pressure and personal safety on an interactive risk-reward model with realistic consequences.

strands.strand: Economic wellbeing, careers and post-16 pathways
strands.gapNote: No app on careers, post-16 choices, financial capability or work readiness.

suggestedApps.title: Pathways Planner
suggestedApps.concept: Interactive map of post-16 routes (A-level, T-level, apprenticeship, work) where the class explores how choices link to careers and earnings.

keyStages.statutoryNote: non-statutory (PSHE/tutorial programme; RSHE statutory duty ends at KS4)

strands.strand: Adult relationships, consent and family/parenting choices
strands.gapNote: No post-16 app on adult relationships, ongoing consent or family/parenting decisions.

suggestedApps.title: Healthy Adult Relationships
suggestedApps.concept: Case-study scenarios on commitment, conflict and consent in adult relationships, with the class debating and ranking responses.

strands.strand: Mental health, independence and life transitions
strands.gapNote: Self-regulation tools transfer but nothing addresses the move to independence, university/work stress or where adults get help.

suggestedApps.title: Transition Resilience Lab
suggestedApps.concept: Simulated first weeks of independent life (money, isolation, workload) where the class builds and stress-tests coping and help-seeking plans.
